    33	## v1.1 patch log (vs. v1)
    34	
    35	| ID | v1 issue | v1.1 fix | Source |
    36	|---|---|---|---|
    37	| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
    38	| C2 | spec did not acknowledge that shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes empty in-memory index → cold-replay impossible | § 0 + § 5 explicit dependency + mitigation: CasStore index persistence is deferred to **CO1.4-extra** (separate atom); v1 documents the gap | Codex Q-H + Gemini Q2 |
    39	| C3 | signing primitive integration via `CanonicalMessage` enum was unspecified; spec called nonexistent digest-form verifier | Path A: extend `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(LedgerEntrySigningPayload)`; sign separate signing payload (NOT raw `LedgerEntry`); new API `keypair.sign_ledger_entry(payload) → SystemSignature` | Codex Q-G + Gemini Q4 |
    40	| K1 | sequencer `next_logical_t.fetch_add(1)` happens BEFORE accept; rejection skips `logical_t`, replay rejects gaps | Dual counter design: `next_submit_id` advances at submit; `next_logical_t` advances ONLY on commit | Codex Q-C |
    70	- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
    71	- L6 signal indices — deferred to **CO1.9**.
    72	- AttributionEngine DAG — deferred to CO P2.4.0 spike (Inv 8 design).
    73	- MetaTx full schema — v4.1 only; v4 emits `MetaProposalDraft` to L3 CAS, not L4.
    74	- **Slash transition** — deferred to CO P2.5 (ChallengeCourt) atom; v4 ledger has no `TxKind::Slash`.
    75	- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
    76	
    77	### What this spec is NOT replacing
    78	- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).

    81	
    82	## § 1 LedgerEntry schema (the stored record)
    83	
    84	```rust
    85	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
    86	
    87	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC2-Append (FC2 transition machinery): canonical record
    88	/// stored at L4. One LedgerEntry per accepted transition. Genesis state has
    89	/// zero LedgerEntries; ledger_root_t = genesis_ledger_root_t (per § 5).
    90	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
    91	pub struct LedgerEntry {
    92	    /// Monotonic counter from sequencer; starts at 1 at first accept.
    93	    /// **K1**: assigned ONLY at commit; rejected submissions never get a logical_t.
    94	    pub logical_t: u64,                          //  1
    95	
    96	    /// Parent state_root before this transition. Equals the resulting_state_root
    97	    /// of the entry at logical_t-1 (or genesis state_root at logical_t=1).
    98	    pub parent_state_root: Hash,                 //  2
    99	
   100	    /// **K2 NEW**: parent_ledger_root before this entry is folded in.
   101	    /// Bound by signature (transplant attack defense). Equals the
   102	    /// resulting_ledger_root of entry at logical_t-1 (or genesis_ledger_root at logical_t=1).
   103	    pub parent_ledger_root: Hash,                //  3
   104	
   105	    /// Discriminator; payload schema depends on this. **K6**: `#[repr(u8)]` for stable
   106	    /// discriminant in canonical digest computation.
   107	    pub tx_kind: TxKind,                         //  4
   108	
   109	    /// CAS handle (CO1.4) to canonically-serialized payload. Sequencer puts payload
   110	    /// to CAS via `CasStore::put(content, object_type, creator, created_at_logical_t, schema_id)`
   250	└── transition_ledger.rs         (NEW; LedgerEntry, LedgerEntrySigningPayload, TxKind, append, replay_*, LedgerWriter)
   251	
   252	src/state/
   253	├── mod.rs                       (existing)
   254	├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
   255	└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
   256	```
   257	
   258	**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).
   259	
   260	---
   261	
   262	## § 3 Sequencer (K1 dual-counter; K3 head_t ownership; C3 sign API)
   263	
   264	```rust
   265	/// TRACE_MATRIX § 5.2.1 — L4 sequencer; single-writer per (runtime_repo, run_id).
   266	pub struct Sequencer {
   267	    /// **K1 NEW**: separate counter for submissions (independent of accept).
   268	    /// Used to derive submit_id for SubmissionReceipt; never appears in LedgerEntry.
   269	    next_submit_id: AtomicU64,
   270	
   271	    /// **K1 changed semantics**: advances ONLY on commit, NOT on submit.
   272	    /// Genesis = 0; first accepted entry gets logical_t=1.
   273	    next_logical_t: AtomicU64,
   274	
   275	    /// **Q1 resolution**: bounded `tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender` (NOT unbounded).
   276	    /// Submit returns `QueueFull` Err on saturation; agents handle backoff.
   277	    queue_tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<TypedTx>,
   278	
   279	    /// CAS handle for payload storage.
   280	    cas: Arc<RwLock<CasStore>>,
   281	
   282	    /// **C3**: signing key handle (CO1.7.0a-f).
   283	    keypair: Arc<Ed25519Keypair>,
   284	    epoch: SystemEpoch,                   // current signing epoch
   285	
   286	    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
   287	    ledger_writer: Arc<RwLock<dyn LedgerWriter>>,
   288	
   289	    /// Predicate + tool registries (read-only).
   290	    predicate_registry: Arc<PredicateRegistry>,
   291	    tool_registry: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
   292	
   293	    /// Current Q_t snapshot.
   294	    q: RwLock<QState>,
   295	}
   296	
   297	impl Sequencer {
   298	    /// Submit a typed transition for processing. Returns immediately with a
   299	    /// SubmissionReceipt carrying `submit_id` (NOT logical_t — submit_id is
   300	    /// always assigned; logical_t only assigned post-accept).
   301	    pub async fn submit(&self, tx: TypedTx) -> Result<SubmissionReceipt, SubmitError>;
   302	
   303	    /// Driver loop: drain queue, run transition, commit on accept. Single-thread internal.
   304	    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<(), SequencerError>;
   305	
   306	    /// Per-tx critical section.
   307	    fn apply_one(&self, tx: TypedTx) -> Result<LedgerEntry, TransitionError> {
   308	        // 1. Snapshot Q_t under read lock
   309	        let q_snapshot = self.q.read().clone();
   310	
   311	        // 2. Dispatch (pure)
   312	        let (q_next, _signals) = dispatch_transition(&q_snapshot, &tx, &self.predicate_registry, &self.tool_registry)?;
   313	        // **K1**: if step returns Err, EARLY RETURN — no logical_t assigned, no entry committed.
   314	
   315	        // 3. Put payload to CAS (DIV-5 5-param signature)
   316	        let mut cas_w = self.cas.write();
   317	        let cas_bytes = canonical_serialize(&tx);  // bincode v2 per § 2.5 of STATE spec
   318	        let payload_cid = cas_w.put(
   319	            &cas_bytes,
   320	            ObjectType::ProposalPayload,  // R2 fix: shipped CAS variant (NOT Transition)
   321	            &format!("sequencer-{}", self.epoch.get()),
   322	            self.next_logical_t.load(Ordering::SeqCst) + 1,  // tentative; final below
   323	            Some("LedgerEntrySigningPayload.v1".to_string()),
   324	        )?;
   325	        drop(cas_w);
   326	
   327	        // 4. **K1**: assign logical_t ONLY now (post-accept)
   328	        let logical_t = self.next_logical_t.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
   329	
   330	        // 5. Build LedgerEntrySigningPayload
   500	| **I-NORANDOM** | tx consuming randomness MUST seed PRNG from `(tx.tx_id, q.state_root_t)` | LedgerEntry.system_signature uses keypair (deterministic); no entropy in append/replay |
   501	
   502	CO1.7 does NOT introduce new invariants — provides machine-checkable witnesses for 8 of the 27 frozen invariants.
   503	
   504	---

   511	| `tests/canonical_digest_stable` | skeleton (v1) | LedgerEntrySigningPayload digest stable across clones; #[repr(u8)] discriminant stable |

   514	| `tests/replay_chain_rejects_parent_state_root_tamper` | skeleton (v1) | ChainOnly replay rejects parent_state_root tamper |
   515	| `tests/replay_chain_rejects_parent_ledger_root_tamper` | **K2 NEW** skeleton (v1) | ChainOnly replay rejects parent_ledger_root tamper (transplant defense) |
   516	| `tests/replay_chain_rejects_ledger_root_tamper` | skeleton (v1) | ChainOnly replay rejects resulting_ledger_root tamper |
   517	| `tests/canonical_digest_excludes_derivatives` | **Q9 NEW** skeleton (v1) | LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest excludes resulting_ledger_root + system_signature; mutation of either does NOT change digest |
   518	| `tests/replay_full_transition_state_root` | CO1.7.5+ (post-CO1.4-extra) | FullTransition replay re-runs dispatch_transition; asserts state_root match (I-DETHASH witness) |
   519	| `tests/system_signature_verifies_via_canonical_message` | CO1.7.5+ | LedgerEntry.system_signature verifies through `verify_system_signature(&CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(...), epoch, pinned_pubkeys)` |
   520	| `tests/cas_payload_round_trip` | CO1.7.5+ (after CO1.4-extra) | put→get round trip; CID stability across runs |
   521	| `tests/sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` | CO1.7.5+ | submit 100 tx; replay → byte-identical state_root |

   621	
   622	## § 14 Honest acknowledgements (v1.1)
   623	
   624	1. CO1.4 CAS API surface verified via type-skeleton smoke 2026-04-28: `CasStore::get(&Cid) → Result<Vec<u8>, CasError>` matches; `CasStore::put` 5-param signature documented (DIV-5).
   625	2. SubmissionQueue choice = bounded tokio::sync::mpsc per Q1 resolution; pivot to different runtime would rewrite `Sequencer.run()`.
   626	3. system_keypair extension (CanonicalMessage variant + sign_ledger_entry API) is additive Wave 4-B extension; non-breaking; verified via type-skeleton (sign API call site is concrete, not unimplemented).
   627	4. **v1.1 patches incorporate 11 round-1 must-fix items + 1 disagreement resolution** (D1: epoch security wins). Detailed in patch log header.
   628	5. **Spec ↔ skeleton divergences cataloged in v1; resolution status v1.1**:
   629	   - DIV-1 CanonicalMessage integration — **resolved** via Q8 (extend enum, Path A) + § 1.2.
   630	   - DIV-2 Q_t mutation API — unchanged status (CO P2.x economy atoms still unblock); skeleton stays unimplemented!() for state mutation.
   631	   - DIV-3 epoch field — **resolved**: in LedgerEntry + bound in LedgerEntrySigningPayload.
   632	   - DIV-4 CasReader → LedgerCasView — **resolved**: narrow trait for replay_full_transition.
   633	   - DIV-5 CasStore::put 5-param — **resolved**: sequencer apply_one stage 3 builds full metadata.
   634	6. **Q9 spec bug found by skeleton smoke** — closed by spec § 1.1: explicit `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` separate struct excludes derivatives.
   635	7. **K2 transplant attack vector** found by Codex round-1 — closed by binding `parent_ledger_root` in signing payload.

    35	| **P5** | "Phase 1 record-only" golden fixture tests asserted only length=64 + self-stability, did NOT lock SHA-256 hex; `TerminalSummary` excluded from round-trip / kind / golden tests | Hardcoded SHA-256 hex constants for all 7 TypedTx fixture digests (Work / Verify / Challenge / Reuse / FinalizeReward / TaskExpire / TerminalSummary). NEW tests: cross-variant non-collision (7×7 pairwise distinct), BTreeSet permutation independence, default round-trip, signing-payload domain non-collision (6 distinct domain digests), signing-payload-excludes-signature (mutating tx.signature must NOT affect digest). All variants now in round-trip + kind-projection. Total typed_tx tests: 11 → 17. | C-2 (Codex Q-J + Gemini Q9) |

handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1:# Gemini CO1.7 transition_ledger Round-2 Audit Run
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8:# Gemini CO1.7 Round-2 Audit
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:34:-   Deferring the implementation of CAS index persistence to a new, dedicated atom (CO1.4-extra) is an architecturally sound decision. It unblocks CO1.7, correctly scopes the work, and documents the dependency, turning an implicit risk into a managed work item.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:50:**Q12 (NEW; scope expansion)**: The expansion is handled correctly. Spec §13 acknowledges the new CO1.4-extra atom and budgets it separately. This is proper dependency management, not scope creep within CO1.7. The approach is acceptable.
handover/audits/GEMINI_WHITEPAPER_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-27_R2.md:53:**Reasoning**: The 6-layer structure of ChainTape is logically clean, with clear separation of concerns. The progression from rules (L1/L2) to data (L3) to history (L4) to current state (L5) to statistical summary (L6) is sound. There is no obvious overlap or missing layer. The explicit note in `v2 § 5.1 Layer 6` confirming that L6 is derivable from L4+L5 correctly addresses `constitution.md Art 0.2` compliance. The appendix (`v2 附录 A`) confirms that Layer 4 correctly subsumes the `CO1.7 transition_ledger` atom.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1:# Gemini CO1.7.5 transition_bodies_and_runtime_wiring Round-1 Audit Run
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:9:# Gemini CO1.7.5 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15:CO1.7.5 demonstrates strong constitutional alignment.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19:-   **Art 0.2 Tape Canonical 公理**: The design upholds this axiom. The `Sequencer::apply_one` pseudocode in the inherited `CO1.7` spec (§ 3) and the shipped `sequencer.rs` (lines 310-315) show that the pure transition function is called *before* any commit action. If it returns an `Err`, the function returns early, and no `LedgerEntry` is committed. This ensures rejected transactions do not advance the ledger state, which holds for all 7 transition bodies as they are all routed through `dispatch_transition`. The `LedgerEntry.system_signature` attests to the sequencer-stamped semantics of the *accepted* transition, fulfilling the attestation requirement.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:21:-   **Art 0.4 Q_t version-controlled**: The design correctly implements the Art 0.4 binding. The spec's D2 deliverable (§ 1 D2) explicitly sets `q.head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)`. This is the direct implementation of Art 0.4's `HEAD_t = git commit SHA` principle. The spec correctly notes in § 0.3.1 that this supersedes the `STATE v1.4 § 3` `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` suggestion, citing the higher authority of `CO1.7 K3 v1.2 § 5`. This demonstrates correct interpretation of the constitutional hierarchy: the specific, audited implementation detail in a later spec (CO1.7) refines the general principle from an earlier one (STATE v1.4), and both align with the highest law (Constitution Art 0.4).
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:29:The spec correctly identifies and carries forward two necessary supersessions from prior, audited specs (`CO1.7-K3-v1.2` and `CO1.1.4-pre1`). The legitimacy of a downstream, more specific, audited spec superseding an upstream, more general one is a valid and necessary principle for architectural evolution. The resolutions themselves are sound.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:35:**This constitutes a process-level challenge**: The spec must be amended to state that as part of the CO1.7.5 atom's closure, a formal change request or issue will be filed against the `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC` to align its pseudocode with the as-implemented reality of `head_t` mutation and `SignalKind` shape.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:41:CO1.7.5 correctly closes Wave 6 #1.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:71:-   **Safe Deferral**: This is a classic example of incremental delivery. It ships a functional L4 now, with a clear and safe path for adding L6 signal richness later. This reduces the complexity and risk of the CO1.7.5 atom.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:109:The CO1.7.5 spec is architecturally sound, constitutionally aligned, and demonstrates a mature understanding of the project's principles. The design correctly closes Wave 6 #1 while providing clear affordances for future waves.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:117:3.  **Clarify Supersession Authority and Responsibility (from Q2)**: The language in § 0.3 should be strengthened. Instead of merely documenting a carry-forward, it should assert the principle that a later, more specific, audited spec (like CO1.7) legitimately supersedes an earlier one (like STATE v1.4), while also acknowledging the institutional debt to file a housekeeping patch against the superseded document. This provides clarity on the project's governance of its own specifications.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:15:Q5: CLOSED as a spec-freeze issue. § 2.5 freezes the canonical serialization rule: bincode v2, big-endian fixed ints, BTreeMap lexicographic keys, UTF-8 length-prefix strings, explicit Option and enum encodings. NEW-5: CLOSED; § 2.5 explicitly defers the full fixture corpus, differential fuzz seed, and complete QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError runner ABI to CO1.1.4-pre1 and CO1.7, so this is tracked downstream rather than left ambiguous. Q6: CLOSED. § 5.2.1 assigns logical_t through sequencer `next_logical_t()`, and § 5.2.6 makes the atomic assignment the canonical tie-break for concurrent submitters.

handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1:# Gemini CO1.7 transition_ledger Round-1 Audit Run
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8:# Gemini CO1.7 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12:The CO1.7 design demonstrates strong alignment with the TuringOS Constitution and the Anti-Oreo architecture.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:33:-   **Preserve WP § 5.L4 axioms?**: **PASS**. The whitepaper defines the logical content of a `WorkTx`. The CO1.7 `LedgerEntry` is an envelope that contains a content-addressed identifier (`tx_payload_cid`) pointing to the canonical `WorkTx` payload. All 12 fields specified in WP § 5.L4 are preserved within the CAS-stored payload. The envelope adds sequencer-level metadata (`logical_t`, `system_signature`, etc.), which is a correct separation of concerns between agent-proposed state and system-accepted state.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:45:-   **A partial Art 0.2 implementation that should NOT be called "I-DETHASH witness" until full?**: **CHALLENGE**. Correct. Calling the current chain-integrity check a full "I-DETHASH witness" is an overstatement. It is a witness for *chain integrity*, not for *deterministic state transition*. The name should be qualified, for example, as an "I-DETHASH chain-integrity witness," until the full replay (spec § 4 `replay` function, including re-running pure transitions) is implemented in CO1.7.5+.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:69:The CO1.7 design is neutral with respect to the final resolution of INV8 (DAG determinism).
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:71:-   The core of the CO1.7 design is the `Sequencer`, which imposes a **strict, linear, total ordering** on all incoming transactions (spec § 3, § 5.2.1). It serializes concurrent submissions into a single, unambiguous sequence based on `logical_t`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:120:The CO1.7 design is constitutionally aligned and architecturally sound in its core principles. However, the audit has identified several significant strategic risks, ambiguities, and integration failures that must be resolved before implementation proceeds beyond the skeleton. The design prioritizes v1 simplicity at the cost of creating a temporary trust model ambiguity (Q3) and lacks explicit forward-compatibility affordances (Q7, Q9). The indirection to CAS (Q2) creates an availability dependency that must be managed. The integration with the existing signing primitive (Q4) is unresolved.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:40:| **P8** (C-3 + GM-2) | FinalizeRewardTx Q-derived discipline + dual-sign rationale | NEW § 4.1 Q-derived (task_id/solver/reward authoritative from Q at replay; royalty NOT on wire); NEW § 4.2 dual-sign (this struct's sig binds payload bytes; envelope sig binds sequencer-stamped bytes; both needed) | Q-derived discipline clearly stated? Replay rule (CO1.7-impl A4) committed? Dual-sign rationale convincing or still redundant? |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:41:| **P9** (GM-1 Gemini Q4) | Cold-replay Art 0.2 | NEW § 0.1 cross-atom ordering gate: v1.1 PASS contingent on CO1.4-extra shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 | Ordering gate explicit + binding? Constitutional commitment strong enough? |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:65:**Q-6. P6 closure (codec wording)**: spec § 7.1 wording now matches bincode-2.0.1 source citations? Decision to keep u32/u64 (not force u8) — is that the right call given >256 variant ceiling never likely to be hit? Any cascaded impact on shipping CO1.7 LedgerEntrySigningPayload digest stability? (CO1.7 commit a03cc52 used same canonical_encode helper.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:67:**Q-7. P8 + P9 closure (Q-derived + dual-sign + cold-replay gate)**: Spec § 4.1 commits replay (CO1.7-impl A4) to Q-derived discipline — is this binding enough? Spec § 0.1 cross-atom ordering gate phrased as "MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra" — strong constitutional commitment or hedged language?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:78:**Q-10. Implementation gating (overall)**: with v1.1 closure, is CO1.7-impl A2 (TypedTx + Sequencer + dispatch_transition) implementable end-to-end against this ABI surface? Specific blockers to call out.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:107:**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:123:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:131:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:135:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:158:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:159:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:160:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:161:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:162:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:171:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:173:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:176:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:177:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:256:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:281:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:289:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:299:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:331:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:337:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:398:| 3+ | … | … | … | iterate to PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:400:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:402:**Audit cost estimate**: ~$15-25 (smaller surface than CO1.7 spec @ $25-42; mostly type definitions + 2 plausibly-derived schemas).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:411:- **Total atom budget**: ~1.5-2.5 days from spec draft to PASS/PASS.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:417:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:419:3. **Versioning**: `extensions: BTreeMap<String, Vec<u8>>` is on `LedgerEntry` (CO1.7); per-tx forward compat is via additive variants on `TypedTx` (e.g. `TypedTx::MetaTx(...)` lands in v4.1). No per-struct `version` field.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2800:| **GM-1** | **Art 0.2 cold-replay constitutional commitment** | Q4: payload data lives in CAS (referenced via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`); CO1.7 spec § 0 already deferred CAS index persistence to **CO1.4-extra**. Until that ships, cold-restart loses payload data → tape is non-canonical → Art 0.2 violation. v1.1 of CO1.1.4-pre1 must explicitly gate PASS on a concrete commitment to ship CO1.4-extra alongside or before CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). Strategic risk: shipping a constitutionally-non-compliant tape layer, even temporarily, is too high. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2827:| **P9**: spec patch — § 0 add explicit "Art 0.2 cold-replay gate": v1.1 PASS contingent on CO1.4-extra commitment (explicit cross-atom dependency; CO1.7-impl A4 must NOT ship before CO1.4-extra) | GM-1 | spec § 0 |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2852:Tracks lower than CO1.7 spec round-1 ($7-12) because spec is shorter. Cumulative project audit spend: ~$143-217 / $890 mid-budget (~16-24%).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2866:5. **Cold-replay → Art 0.2 commitment is a real cross-atom gate**: declaring "CO1.4-extra is a separate atom" is not enough; the deferred dependency creates a window where the tape is non-canonical. Sedimented: v1.1 must explicitly state the cross-atom ordering constraint (CO1.4-extra MUST ship before CO1.7-impl A4 replay_full_transition) and PASS is contingent on that ordering being honored.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2883:> - **§ 2.5 + § 7 fixture corpus defer-ack** (Q5/NEW-5): canonical serialization RULE frozen v1.4; full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzz seed lands in CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7 atoms (not v1.x spec scope).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3198:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7480:    20	| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7488:    28	**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7492:    32	**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7515:    55	- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7516:    56	- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7517:    57	- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7518:    58	- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7519:    59	- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7528:    68	**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7530:    70	**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7533:    73	- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7534:    74	- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7604:   178	If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7612:   186	- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7622:   196	`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7629:   234	`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7722:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7909:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:153:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:9185:Keeping u32/u64 is the right call. Forcing u8 would require custom serde and would rotate all TypedTx payload CIDs. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:408:## § 6 Invariants enforced by CO1.7
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:410:| ID | Invariant | Enforced where in CO1.7 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:418:| **I-NOENV** | step_transition dependency tree has no `std::env` access | grep test in CO1.7 module — already enforced by CLAUDE.md hardcoded-config rule (C-027) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:421:CO1.7 does NOT introduce new invariants — it provides the machine-checkable witness for 8 of the 27 frozen invariants.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:429:| `tests/transition_determinism.rs` | step_transition(q, tx) called twice → byte-identical Q_{t+1}; ledger_root_t identical (CO1.7 append() witness) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:430:| `tests/q_state_reconstruct.rs` | Run N transitions live → snapshot Q_t. Cold-restart, call replay(genesis, [entries]) → assert state_root + ledger_root match snapshot. (CO1.7 replay() witness) |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:432:| `tests/finalize_batch_order.rs` | 3 claims expire same tick; assert ordering by (expires_at, claim_id); 2 runs byte-identical (CO1.7 sequencer + § 5.2.3 witness) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:434:| `tests/ledger_root_chain_integrity.rs` | NEW, CO1.7-specific: tamper with one LedgerEntry's resulting_ledger_root; replay must FAIL with LedgerRootMismatch at that index |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:435:| `tests/cas_payload_recovery.rs` | NEW, CO1.7-specific: serialize a WorkTx → CAS put → LedgerEntry references CID → CAS get → byte-identical WorkTx |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:438:**Total CO1.7-specific tests**: 3 NEW + 5 referenced from spec § 4 = 8 conformance tests.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:444:CO1.7 publishes a single function `dispatch_transition(q, typed_tx) -> (q_next, signals)` that the sequencer's `apply_one` calls. Existing transition functions in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3-3.7` are wired into this dispatch:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:460:**Where the transition function bodies live**: this is decided per-atom downstream (CO1.7.5 implements `step_transition`; CO1.7.6 implements verify/challenge/etc. — see Plan v3.2 § 3.4 atoms). CO1.7 itself only ships the dispatch + sequencer + ledger writer; the transition function bodies are stubs (`unimplemented!()`) that downstream atoms fill.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:466:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wal.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required**. Direct edit on `main` is per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:468:The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is **NOT in CO1.7 scope** — it is in CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split) per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3` Legacy Economic Tx Disposition table.

The progression from rules (L1/L2) to data (L3) to history (L4) to current state (L5) to statistical summary (L6) is sound. There is no obvious overlap or missing layer. The explicit note in `v2 § 5.1 Layer 6` confirming that L6 is derivable from L4+L5 correctly addresses `constitution.md Art 0.2` compliance. Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6315:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:466:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). Direct edit on `main` is per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard".

The progression from Layer 0 (Constitution Root) to Layer 6 (Signal Indices) follows a clear dependency path from trust anchor to derived analytics. There is no significant overlap between layers. Layer 4 (Transition Ledger) correctly serves as the append-only source of truth from which Layer 5 (Materialized State) and Layer 6 are derived. The appendix (`附录 A`) confirms that Layer 4 is intended to subsume the `CO1.7 transition_ledger` atom.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6937:# Codex CO1.7 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6942:DIV-2 real but overstated as an L4 blocker. `QState` economic fields are currently BTreeMap newtype shells, not domain mutation APIs ([q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:143), [q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:157)). CO1.7 can still implement ledger append/storage and chain replay; full economic transition bodies remain blocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6967:Partial replay is acceptable only as skeleton smoke, not as a v1 CO1.7 deliverable. Spec says replay is the I-DETHASH witness at lines 219-264 and invariant witness at lines 305-306. Skeleton explicitly does chain-only replay and does not verify signatures, payload recovery, or transition recomputation ([transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:240), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:270)).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6974:CO1.7 can own ledger roots and commit-chain heads. L5 must own state_root materialization. Spec v1.1 should define a stub `StateRootProvider`/materializer boundary or defer `head_t` mutation out of CO1.7.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6993:CAS cold replay is not actually supported by shipped `CasStore`: `open()` initializes an empty in-memory index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:67)), and `get()` requires that index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:111)). CO1.7 replay cannot recover payloads after restart unless CAS metadata is persisted or ledger commits include enough blob lookup data.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7001:Conformance gap is large: spec promises 8 CO1.7 tests lines 318-331; skeleton has 6 inline unit tests and none for signature, CAS recovery, sequencer serialization, full replay, or canonical fixtures.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7018:# Codex CO1.7 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7023:DIV-2 real but overstated as an L4 blocker. `QState` economic fields are currently BTreeMap newtype shells, not domain mutation APIs ([q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:143), [q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:157)). CO1.7 can still implement ledger append/storage and chain replay; full economic transition bodies remain blocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7048:Partial replay is acceptable only as skeleton smoke, not as a v1 CO1.7 deliverable. Spec says replay is the I-DETHASH witness at lines 219-264 and invariant witness at lines 305-306. Skeleton explicitly does chain-only replay and does not verify signatures, payload recovery, or transition recomputation ([transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:240), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:270)).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7055:CO1.7 can own ledger roots and commit-chain heads. L5 must own state_root materialization. Spec v1.1 should define a stub `StateRootProvider`/materializer boundary or defer `head_t` mutation out of CO1.7.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7074:CAS cold replay is not actually supported by shipped `CasStore`: `open()` initializes an empty in-memory index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:67)), and `get()` requires that index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:111)). CO1.7 replay cannot recover payloads after restart unless CAS metadata is persisted or ledger commits include enough blob lookup data.

PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md (round-4 PASS/PASS, frozen) + PREREG_AMENDMENT_p0_defer_2026-04-25.md (Trust Root entry 25) are the contracts you're auditing against.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:184:**Q8. PASS gate**: does v1.2 close all R2 findings cleanly enough for CO1.7-impl A2 unblock?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:211:**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:245:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:253:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:257:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:280:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:281:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:282:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:283:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:284:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:293:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:295:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:298:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:299:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:378:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:403:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:411:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:421:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:453:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:459:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:543:| 4+ | … | … | … | iterate to PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:545:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:547:**Audit cost estimate**: ~$15-25 (smaller surface than CO1.7 spec @ $25-42; mostly type definitions + 2 plausibly-derived schemas).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:556:- **Total atom budget**: ~1.5-2.5 days from spec draft to PASS/PASS.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:562:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:564:3. **Versioning**: `extensions: BTreeMap<String, Vec<u8>>` is on `LedgerEntry` (CO1.7); per-tx forward compat is via additive variants on `TypedTx` (e.g. `TypedTx::MetaTx(...)` lands in v4.1). No per-struct `version` field.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:21:# Codex Audit — CO1.7-impl BUNDLE Round-1 (Pre-CO1.7.5 implementation gate)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:25:**Mandate**: round-1 dual external audit on the **CO1.7-impl bundle** (A1 + A2 + A3 + A4) + **CO1.4-extra** atom, all mid-implementation commits. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + spec § 12: this PASS/PASS gates entry to CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring to bus.rs/kernel.rs).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:34:| **A2** Sequencer | `609d8d5` | ~290 (in 430 incl. tests) | 5 | K1 dual counter (next_submit_id / next_logical_t); tokio mpsc bounded queue; apply_one 9 stages per spec § 3; K3 head_t deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring; ApplyError wrapper (impl deviation from spec § 3 line 307 — TransitionError stays closed-taxonomy + additive-only per CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:35:| **A3** dispatch_transition | `609d8d5` | ~30 | 1 | Exhaustive 7-variant enum match; every variant returns NotYetImplemented stub (CO1.7.5 fills) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:36:| **A4** replay_full_transition | `272fcf4` | ~140 + LedgerCasView trait | 4 (1 #[ignore]) | 9-stage full-mode replay; new ReplayError::Transition variant; LedgerCasView trait (DIV-4 closure); tests prove stages 1-6 PASS, stage 7 stubs; #[ignore]'d test for full state_root reconstruction (CO1.7.5) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:37:| **CO1.4-extra** | `b6b7574` | ~110 + tests | 5 | Sidecar JSONL CAS index persistence; closes Art 0.2 cold-replay gate per CO1.1.4-pre1 § 0.1 cross-atom ordering |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:43:- `CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md` v1.2 — pre-implementation PASS/PASS (rounds 1-3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:44:- `CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md` v1.2.2 — ABI surface PASS/PASS (rounds 1-5)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:49:- **PASS/PASS** unblocks **CO1.7.5** (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring to bus.rs/kernel.rs); CO1.7.5 is the final L4 atom
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:57:**Q-B. A2 Sequencer K1 invariant + K3 deferred head_t**: K1 — submit advances submit_id; commit advances logical_t. Verify rejected-submission path does NOT advance logical_t (test `apply_one_stub_does_not_consume_logical_t` covers stub mode; what about future real-transition rejection path)? K3 — head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+. Is leaving `q.head_t` un-mutated (still at QState::default empty string) during the entire CO1.7-impl runtime ACCEPTABLE for Art 0.4 (Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩) compliance? Or is this a gap that should at least set head_t to a placeholder pointing at the latest commit OID?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:63:**Q-E. A3 dispatch_transition exhaustive match contract**: every variant returns NotYetImplemented. Is this the right pattern for "transition body not yet implemented", or should the stubs use unimplemented!() macro for clearer crash semantics? (Tests verify the Err return path; CO1.7.5 fills bodies preserving the match arms.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:78:- Test gaps: anything from STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 4 (27 invariants) that CO1.7-impl bundle should provide a witness for but doesn't?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:81:**Q-L. Implementation gating**: with bundle at 237/0 PASS, is CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition bodies + STEP_B bus.rs/kernel.rs wiring) implementable end-to-end against this bundle? Specific blockers to call out.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:85:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:108:# CO1.7 spec v1.2 (already PASS/PASS)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:110:# CO1.7 Transition Ledger v1.2 — Round-2 closure
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:112:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:117:**Round-1 verdicts**: `handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md` + `handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md`; merged in `handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:120:- `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` v1.4 — typed schemas + step_transition pseudocode + 27 invariants (round-4 PASS/PASS)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:121:- `SYSTEM_KEYPAIR_SECURITY_v1_2026-04-27.md` — runtime keypair lifecycle (CO1.7.0a-f, done @ Wave 4-B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:133:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:134:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:146:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:151:| K3 | L4/L5 head_t ownership inconsistent (spec line 194 vs 276 disagreed) | CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + commit-chain `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` only; L5 (CO1.8) owns `state_root_t` mutation; sequencer drops `head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(...)` line | Codex Q-E |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:152:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:155:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:165:### In scope (CO1.7 atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:171:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:172:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:178:- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:179:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:184:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:187:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:316:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:358:├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:363:├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:364:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:367:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:391:    /// **C3**: signing key handle (CO1.7.0a-f).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:395:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:487:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:499:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:524:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:539:/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:570:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:588:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:592:**Implementation (CO1.7.5+)**: `Git2LedgerWriter` (built on existing CO1.4 CAS); skeleton `InMemoryLedgerWriter` for v1 testing.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:611:CO1.7 does NOT introduce new invariants — provides machine-checkable witnesses for 8 of the 27 frozen invariants.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:629:| `tests/cas_payload_round_trip` | CO1.7.5+ (after CO1.4-extra) | put→get round trip; CID stability across runs |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:632:**v1 stage (skeleton)**: 8 tests (6 already in skeleton + 2 NEW K2/Q9). **CO1.7.5+ stage**: 4 more.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:664:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:671:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:673:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:697:| Q6 replay error mode | open | **Reject on first error** (current). Diagnostic-collection mode is a v4.x extension; first-error simplicity matches CO1.7.5 implementation budget. |

All addressed. |
