handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:786:# CO1.1.4-pre1 spec v1.2.2 (already PASS/PASS)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:792:**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:826:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:834:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:838:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:861:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:862:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:863:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:864:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:865:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:874:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:876:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:879:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:880:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:959:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:984:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:992:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1002:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1034:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1040:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11745:/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"apply_one|head_t|LedgerWriter|ReplayError|replay_full_transition|append\\(|ObjectType::ProposalPayload|K3|ApplyError|FullTransition|Json|JSONL|sidecar|cold-replay|TransitionError\" handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11749:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11804:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:39:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11805:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:47:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11806:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:51:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11807:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:76:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11808:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:78:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11809:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:87:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11810:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:89:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11811:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:93:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11812:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:197:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11813:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:247:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11816:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:3:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11817:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:16:**Single sentence**: implement the L4 transition_ledger module so that `ledger::append(parent_root, signing_digest) → new_root` (called from sequencer) is real code, the L4 sequencer (§ 5.2.1) is real code, and `Q_t.ledger_root_t` is no longer a placeholder.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11818:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:25:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11819:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:26:| **R2-C2-CAS** | Spec § 3 sequencer pseudocode used `ObjectType::Transition` but shipped `ObjectType` has no such variant | Spec § 3 changed to `ObjectType::ProposalPayload` (the existing variant for agent work_tx payloads — semantically correct, no schema extension needed) | Codex round-2 must-fix #3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11820:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:37:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11821:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:38:| C2 | spec did not acknowledge that shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes empty in-memory index → cold-replay impossible | § 0 + § 5 explicit dependency + mitigation: CasStore index persistence is deferred to **CO1.4-extra** (separate atom); v1 documents the gap | Codex Q-H + Gemini Q2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11822:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:42:| K3 | L4/L5 head_t ownership inconsistent (spec line 194 vs 276 disagreed) | CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + commit-chain `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` only; L5 (CO1.8) owns `state_root_t` mutation; sequencer drops `head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(...)` line | Codex Q-E |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11823:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:43:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11824:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:61:- **append(parent_root, signing_digest)**: pure function returning the new ledger_root.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11825:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:62:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11826:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:63:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11827:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:70:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11828:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:75:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11829:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:115:    /// — accepted as-returned from the transition function (K3 boundary).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11842:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:380:        // alongside Hash). Until then, head_t remains at QState placeholder; replay
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11843:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:381:        // and chain-integrity tests do NOT depend on head_t.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11844:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:388:**Why dual counter (K1)**: rejection of a submission must NOT consume a logical_t, because (a) skeleton's `InMemoryLedgerWriter::commit` enforces `expected_logical_t = len + 1` and would reject a gap; (b) replay enforces `entry.logical_t == (i+1)` and would reject a gap. Submitter IDs (`submit_id`) are returned from `submit()` immediately for receipt; logical_t is observable only on the committed entry.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11845:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:390:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11846:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:392:**Q3 (Gemini)**: `Sequencer` vs `LedgerWriter + OrderingCoordinator` split — v1.1 keeps `Sequencer` as the abstraction; trait-segregation refactor is a v4.x consideration (the current single-writer constraint per § 5.2.1 makes the split synthetic for v1).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11854:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:439:**I-DETHASH witness (revised per C1)**: `replay_full_transition` is the I-DETHASH witness. `replay_chain_integrity` is necessary-but-not-sufficient — passing chain check does NOT prove transition determinism. v1 documents this explicitly to close trust ambiguity.

That drops all non-root genesis state: budget, registries, balances, task markets. CO1.7.5 cannot reconstruct real state from this API.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12270:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit

That drops all non-root genesis state: budget, registries, balances, task markets. CO1.7.5 cannot reconstruct real state from this API.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12332:CO1.7.5 is close, but not gate-ready. The transition bodies can be written, but full replay and sequencer commit semantics need the above patches first.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1:# Gemini CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit Run
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8:# Gemini CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15:- **Art 0.4 Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩ version-controlled**: The implementation explicitly does NOT mutate `head_t`, leaving it at `QState::default` (`sequencer.rs:356-361`). The CO1.7 spec v1.2 § 3 explicitly defers this mutation to CO1.7.5+ wiring (R2-K3 closure). While this is a temporary deviation from the full constitutional vision of Art 0.4, the implementation correctly adheres to the audited spec. The deviation is documented, scoped, and has a planned resolution. It is an acceptable interim state, not an implementation defect. **PASS**.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:26:The primary "missing" piece is the body of the transition functions themselves (`dispatch_transition` stubs), which is explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5. This bundle delivers the L4 *framework* or *scaffolding* as envisioned in WP § 5. The stubs act as a clear contract for the next atom. There are no visible omissions in the L4 machinery itself that would surface as a structural gap in Wave 6. **PASS**.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:37:The deferral of `head_t` mutation presents a latent risk of observable inconsistency. For the duration of CO1.7-impl runtime, any component querying `q.head_t` will receive a stale, default empty string.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:39:The CO1.7 spec v1.2 § 3 mitigates this by stating, "replay and chain-integrity tests do NOT depend on head_t." The provided implementation upholds this; `replay_full_transition` reconstructs state without referencing `head_t`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:41:The risk becomes acute if CO1.7.5 is delayed or if parallel atoms are developed that *do* assume a valid `head_t`. The state is acceptable *only* as a transient, short-lived condition with a hard dependency on CO1.7.5 for resolution. The implementation is correct per the spec, but the strategic risk is significant enough to warrant a **CHALLENGE**. The system is in a temporarily non-constitutional state, and while planned, this must be tracked with the highest priority.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:46:## Q6 CO1.7.5 unblock contract
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:47:The next atom, CO1.7.5, has a clear contract based on this bundle:
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:50:2.  **Wire `head_t` mutation**: Implement the logic that calls `Git2LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid()` after a successful commit and updates `q_w.head_t` with the new commit SHA. Per CO1.7 spec § 3, this wiring happens *outside* the L4 sequencer's `apply_one` path, likely in the main runtime loop that drives the sequencer.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:52:The current bundle does not present any visible omissions that would block CO1.7.5. The `LedgerWriter` trait's return of `Hash` and the separate `Git2LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid()` method is a workable, if slightly indirect, interface for CO1.7.5 to consume. The path is clear. **PASS**.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:67:1.  **`head_t` Deferral Debt**: The most critical risk. The temporary constitutional deviation regarding `head_t` is now concrete in the code. This creates a high-priority technical debt that, if not paid promptly in CO1.7.5, could lead to subtle state inconsistencies as other system components are built.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:76:1.  **Must-Fix: `head_t` Constitutional Alignment.** The CHALLENGE can only be resolved when CO1.7.5 is delivered, implementing the `head_t` mutation to reflect the true git commit SHA per Art 0.4. This is the highest priority architectural item.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:119:**Q8. PASS gate**: does v1.2 close all R2 findings cleanly enough for CO1.7-impl A2 unblock?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:146:**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:180:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:188:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:192:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:215:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:216:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:217:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:218:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:219:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:228:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:230:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:233:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:234:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:313:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:338:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:346:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:356:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:388:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:394:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:478:| 4+ | … | … | … | iterate to PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:480:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:482:**Audit cost estimate**: ~$15-25 (smaller surface than CO1.7 spec @ $25-42; mostly type definitions + 2 plausibly-derived schemas).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:491:- **Total atom budget**: ~1.5-2.5 days from spec draft to PASS/PASS.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:497:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:499:3. **Versioning**: `extensions: BTreeMap<String, Vec<u8>>` is on `LedgerEntry` (CO1.7); per-tx forward compat is via additive variants on `TypedTx` (e.g. `TypedTx::MetaTx(...)` lands in v4.1). No per-struct `version` field.

handover/audits/CODEX_WHITEPAPER_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:43:Layer 4 correctly points at the deferred transition-ledger atom: v2 defines Transition Ledger as an append-only state-transition ledger with tx fields (`handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_v4_WHITEPAPER_v2_2026-04-27_ANTI_OREO_RESTORATION.md:359-385`), and Appendix A maps §5.1 Layer 4 to CO1.7 transition_ledger (`handover/whitepapers/TURINGOS_v4_WHITEPAPER_v2_2026-04-27_ANTI_OREO_RESTORATION.md:1327-1333`).
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:68:- Blocks CO P1? Yes for CO1.1.4/CO1.1.5 and CO1.7; otherwise hidden-input rot can be moved into new modules under a passing test name.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:122:- Blocks CO P1? Yes for CO1.1.4/CO1.1.5 and CO1.7. The gix concurrency spike can proceed only if treated as substrate preflight, not as proof of transition determinism.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:130:- CO1.7 is in Phase 1 and includes transition ledger/retry metadata at `handover/architect-insights/CO_MEGA_PLAN_v3.2_2026-04-27.md:191-204`; CO P2.0a `i64 micro-coin` is scheduled only after P1 at `handover/architect-insights/CO_MEGA_PLAN_v3.2_2026-04-27.md:208-210`.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:135:- CO1.7 implements `TransitionTx` with `i64`, while CO1.1.4 keeps bus/kernel APIs in `f64`. A later P2.0a migration changes serialization and hashes for already-written transition records, breaking replay and STEP_B comparison.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:138:- Move `MicroCoin(i64)`, `StakeMicroCoin`, and `RoyaltyRatePpm` to a P1 prerequisite atom before CO1.7 and before any STEP_B branch serializes tx records.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:140:- Blocks CO P1? Blocks spec freeze and CO1.7. Early non-money scaffolding can proceed only if the plan is amended before launch to move the type prerequisite into P1.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:153:- The dependency graph says CO1.1.4 is blocked by spec freeze at `handover/architect-insights/SPRINT_DEPENDENCY_GRAPH_v1_2026-04-27.md:94-96`, CO1.1.5 is blocked by CO1.1.4 at `handover/architect-insights/SPRINT_DEPENDENCY_GRAPH_v1_2026-04-27.md:97-99`, and CO1.7.5 depends on the transition schema/QState stack at `handover/architect-insights/SPRINT_DEPENDENCY_GRAPH_v1_2026-04-27.md:103-108`.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:160:- Amend the sprint dependency graph so `MicroCoin` lands before CO1.7 and before any serialized tx fixture.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:185:   - Blocks CO P1? Yes for CO1.7 and STEP_B.
handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_FREEZE_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:214:NO-GO for CO P1 launch as a spec-freeze gate. Limited read-only or spike work may continue, but CO1.1.4, CO1.1.5, and CO1.7 must not start against this spec until the required amendments are made and re-audited.

Do not modify after Phase A5 commit gate except via formal addendum (`PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26_ADDENDUM_*.md`) which itself requires dual external audit.

handover/audits/GEMINI_WHITEPAPER_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:56:The 6-layer structure of ChainTape is logically clean and comprehensive. The progression from Layer 0 (Constitution Root) to Layer 6 (Signal Indices) follows a clear dependency path from trust anchor to derived analytics. There is no significant overlap between layers. Layer 4 (Transition Ledger) correctly serves as the append-only source of truth from which Layer 5 (Materialized State) and Layer 6 are derived. The appendix (`附录 A`) confirms that Layer 4 is intended to subsume the `CO1.7 transition_ledger` atom.

C-027 env-overridable KDF params. Restricted typed sign API. | est ~$15 | n/a (impl, not audit) | Atom delivered. Test count 237 → 244 (+5 conformance + 2 inline); 0 fail. Single audited unsafe (mlock); no log noise. |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:164:### In scope (CO1.7 atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:170:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:177:- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:178:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + `head_t`; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:183:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:186:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:315:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:347:├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:352:├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:353:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:356:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:470:        // **K3**: do NOT mutate q_w.head_t here. CO1.7's ownership is `ledger_root_t` only;
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:474:        // path is CO1.7.5+ wiring concern.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:483:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` and the commit-chain `head_t`; CO1.8 (L5 materializer) owns `state_root_t` mutation. Sequencer accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as the transition function returns it; sequencer does NOT call `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1595:**What § 5.2.7 leaves to implementation**: queue type (mpsc / lock-free / mutex+VecDeque), executor (tokio / std::thread), back-pressure policy. CO1.7 v1 picks tokio mpsc (matches existing kernel runtime). Round-1 audit may push back.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1693:## § 6 Invariants enforced by CO1.7
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1695:| ID | Invariant | Enforced where in CO1.7 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1703:| **I-NOENV** | step_transition dependency tree has no `std::env` access | grep test in CO1.7 module — already enforced by CLAUDE.md hardcoded-config rule (C-027) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1706:CO1.7 does NOT introduce new invariants — it provides the machine-checkable witness for 8 of the 27 frozen invariants.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1714:| `tests/transition_determinism.rs` | step_transition(q, tx) called twice → byte-identical Q_{t+1}; ledger_root_t identical (CO1.7 append() witness) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1715:| `tests/q_state_reconstruct.rs` | Run N transitions live → snapshot Q_t. Cold-restart, call replay(genesis, [entries]) → assert state_root + ledger_root match snapshot. (CO1.7 replay() witness) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1716:| `tests/l4_sequencer_serialization.rs` | Submit 100 tx concurrently from 8 threads; assert (logical_t, tx_id) is strict total order; replay produces deterministic state_root (CO1.7 sequencer witness) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1717:| `tests/finalize_batch_order.rs` | 3 claims expire same tick; assert ordering by (expires_at, claim_id); 2 runs byte-identical (CO1.7 sequencer + § 5.2.3 witness) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1719:| `tests/ledger_root_chain_integrity.rs` | NEW, CO1.7-specific: tamper with one LedgerEntry's resulting_ledger_root; replay must FAIL with LedgerRootMismatch at that index |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1720:| `tests/cas_payload_recovery.rs` | NEW, CO1.7-specific: serialize a WorkTx → CAS put → LedgerEntry references CID → CAS get → byte-identical WorkTx |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1721:| `tests/system_signature_verifies.rs` | NEW, CO1.7-specific: every committed LedgerEntry's system_signature verifies against the committed system_keypair public key |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1723:**Total CO1.7-specific tests**: 3 NEW + 5 referenced from spec § 4 = 8 conformance tests.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1729:CO1.7 publishes a single function `dispatch_transition(q, typed_tx) -> (q_next, signals)` that the sequencer's `apply_one` calls. Existing transition functions in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3-3.7` are wired into this dispatch:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1745:**Where the transition function bodies live**: this is decided per-atom downstream (CO1.7.5 implements `step_transition`; CO1.7.6 implements verify/challenge/etc. — see Plan v3.2 § 3.4 atoms). CO1.7 itself only ships the dispatch + sequencer + ledger writer; the transition function bodies are stubs (`unimplemented!()`) that downstream atoms fill.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1751:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wal.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required**. Direct edit on `main` is per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1753:The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is **NOT in CO1.7 scope** — it is in CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split) per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3` Legacy Economic Tx Disposition table.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1788:| 2+ | … | … | … | iterate to PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1790:**Pre-implementation gate**: CO1.7 v1 must reach `PASS/PASS` from Codex + Gemini before any `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` or `src/state/sequencer.rs` code is written. Sedimented per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" (Generator ≠ Evaluator) + memory `feedback_dual_audit`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1807:4. ~~system_signature integration relies on CO1.7.0a-f's API exactly as shipped~~ — verified post smoke: `SystemSignature::from_bytes`, `SystemEpoch::new/get`, `verify_system_signature(sig, msg, epoch, pinned_pubkeys)` all public. The actual `CanonicalMessage` enum has 3 fixed variants, LedgerEntry is NOT among them. **Q8** (NEW) surfaced.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2248:> - **§ 2.5 + § 7 fixture corpus defer-ack** (Q5/NEW-5): canonical serialization RULE frozen v1.4; full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzz seed lands in CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7 atoms (not v1.x spec scope).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2563:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7587:./handover/audits/run_gemini_co1_7_round1_audit.py:129:    f.write(f"# Gemini CO1.7 transition_ledger Round-1 Audit Run\n")
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7588:./handover/audits/GEMINI_WHITEPAPER_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-27_R2.md:53:**Reasoning**: The 6-layer structure of ChainTape is logically clean, with clear separation of concerns. The progression from rules (L1/L2) to data (L3) to history (L4) to current state (L5) to statistical summary (L6) is sound. There is no obvious overlap or missing layer. The explicit note in `v2 § 5.1 Layer 6` confirming that L6 is derivable from L4+L5 correctly addresses `constitution.md Art 0.2` compliance. The appendix (`v2 附录 A`) confirms that Layer 4 correctly subsumes the `CO1.7 transition_ledger` atom.

Verify against `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` + memory `feedback_step_b_protocol`. Are there indirect modifications (e.g., changes to bus.rs/kernel.rs/wal.rs that the sequencer integration WILL force later)?

Implementation gating**: assuming all your CHALLENGEs are addressed in v1.1, is the joint artifact implementable end-to-end (i.e., `cargo test --lib transition_ledger` will pass with stubs filled in by CO1.7.5+)? Specific blockers to call out.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7595:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:119:**Single sentence**: implement the L4 transition_ledger module so that `ledger::append(parent_root, tx) → new_root` (called from § 3 transition pseudocode) is real code, the L4 sequencer (§ 5.2.1) is real code, and `Q_t.ledger_root_t` is no longer a placeholder.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7596:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:143:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7597:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:214:├── mod.rs                       (re-exports; existing — extends with `pub mod transition_ledger`)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7598:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:216:└── transition_ledger.rs         (NEW, this atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7599:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:224:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` is in `bottom_white::ledger` because it is a tool layer (storage); `sequencer` is in `state::` because it touches Q_t mutation. Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7600:./handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:466:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wal.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required**. Direct edit on `main` is per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard".

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8200:     8	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC2-Append + WP § 5.L4: L4 transition ledger (CO1.7 type skeleton).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8222:# Codex CO1.7 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8227:DIV-2 real but overstated as an L4 blocker. `QState` economic fields are currently BTreeMap newtype shells, not domain mutation APIs ([q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:143), [q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:157)). CO1.7 can still implement ledger append/storage and chain replay; full economic transition bodies remain blocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8252:Partial replay is acceptable only as skeleton smoke, not as a v1 CO1.7 deliverable. Spec says replay is the I-DETHASH witness at lines 219-264 and invariant witness at lines 305-306. Skeleton explicitly does chain-only replay and does not verify signatures, payload recovery, or transition recomputation ([transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:240), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:270)).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8259:CO1.7 can own ledger roots and commit-chain heads. L5 must own state_root materialization. Spec v1.1 should define a stub `StateRootProvider`/materializer boundary or defer `head_t` mutation out of CO1.7.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8278:CAS cold replay is not actually supported by shipped `CasStore`: `open()` initializes an empty in-memory index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:67)), and `get()` requires that index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:111)). CO1.7 replay cannot recover payloads after restart unless CAS metadata is persisted or ledger commits include enough blob lookup data.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8286:Conformance gap is large: spec promises 8 CO1.7 tests lines 318-331; skeleton has 6 inline unit tests and none for signature, CAS recovery, sequencer serialization, full replay, or canonical fixtures.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8307:# Gemini CO1.7 transition_ledger Round-1 Audit Run
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8314:# Gemini CO1.7 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8318:The CO1.7 design demonstrates strong alignment with the TuringOS Constitution and the Anti-Oreo architecture.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8339:-   **Preserve WP § 5.L4 axioms?**: **PASS**. The whitepaper defines the logical content of a `WorkTx`. The CO1.7 `LedgerEntry` is an envelope that contains a content-addressed identifier (`tx_payload_cid`) pointing to the canonical `WorkTx` payload. All 12 fields specified in WP § 5.L4 are preserved within the CAS-stored payload. The envelope adds sequencer-level metadata (`logical_t`, `system_signature`, etc.), which is a correct separation of concerns between agent-proposed state and system-accepted state.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8351:-   **A partial Art 0.2 implementation that should NOT be called "I-DETHASH witness" until full?**: **CHALLENGE**. Correct. Calling the current chain-integrity check a full "I-DETHASH witness" is an overstatement. It is a witness for *chain integrity*, not for *deterministic state transition*. The name should be qualified, for example, as an "I-DETHASH chain-integrity witness," until the full replay (spec § 4 `replay` function, including re-running pure transitions) is implemented in CO1.7.5+.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8375:The CO1.7 design is neutral with respect to the final resolution of INV8 (DAG determinism).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8377:-   The core of the CO1.7 design is the `Sequencer`, which imposes a **strict, linear, total ordering** on all incoming transactions (spec § 3, § 5.2.1). It serializes concurrent submissions into a single, unambiguous sequence based on `logical_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8426:The CO1.7 design is constitutionally aligned and architecturally sound in its core principles. However, the audit has identified several significant strategic risks, ambiguities, and integration failures that must be resolved before implementation proceeds beyond the skeleton. The design prioritizes v1 simplicity at the cost of creating a temporary trust model ambiguity (Q3) and lacks explicit forward-compatibility affordances (Q7, Q9). The indirection to CAS (Q2) creates an availability dependency that must be managed. The integration with the existing signing primitive (Q4) is unresolved.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8447:# CO1.7 Round-1 Dual External Audit — Merged Verdict
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8450:**Target**: spec v1 (`handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md`) + type-skeleton (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8484:| **K3** | **L4/L5 head_t ownership inconsistent** — spec line 194 (`from_state_root`) vs line 276 (`commit_sha`) disagree | Q-E | CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + commit-chain head; L5 (CO1.8) owns `state_root_t` materialization. Spec v1.1 explicit boundary; defer `head_t` mutation OR define `StateRootProvider` stub. |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8557:| **A** | Execute v1.1 patches now (Tier 1+2+3) → round-2 audit | ~3-4 hr total to PASS/PASS gate; recommended |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10597:    47	- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10604:    54	- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10605:    55	- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + `head_t`; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10610:    60	- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10613:    63	- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12534:# Codex CO1.7 Round-2 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12542:- **Q-A6 K3 L4/L5 boundary: PARTIAL.** `from_state_root` is removed, but §3 still says `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` while `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash`, not commit SHA; see spec [§3](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:347) and [§5](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:438).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12543:- **Q-A7 K4 trait: CLOSED.** `commit(&mut self) -> Hash` matches skeleton; `iter_from` deferral is OK for CO1.7.5+.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12551:- **Q-B1: NEW-ISSUE.** The CAS metadata timing is not racy under the stated single-driver sequencer, but spec §3 uses `ObjectType::Transition` at [line 295](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:293); shipped `ObjectType` has no such variant in [schema.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/schema.rs:42). Epoch in `creator` is not a material leak because epoch is public in the entry.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12567:2. **Fix the K3 head_t/commit return contradiction.** Either make `LedgerWriter::commit` return a commit `NodeId`/commit result, or explicitly defer `head_t` mutation and remove the “CO1.7 owns head_t now” claim from spec §0/§3/§5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12575:# Codex CO1.7 Round-2 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12583:- **Q-A6 K3 L4/L5 boundary: PARTIAL.** `from_state_root` is removed, but §3 still says `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` while `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash`, not commit SHA; see spec [§3](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:347) and [§5](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:438).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12584:- **Q-A7 K4 trait: CLOSED.** `commit(&mut self) -> Hash` matches skeleton; `iter_from` deferral is OK for CO1.7.5+.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12592:- **Q-B1: NEW-ISSUE.** The CAS metadata timing is not racy under the stated single-driver sequencer, but spec §3 uses `ObjectType::Transition` at [line 295](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:293); shipped `ObjectType` has no such variant in [schema.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/schema.rs:42). Epoch in `creator` is not a material leak because epoch is public in the entry.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12608:2. **Fix the K3 head_t/commit return contradiction.** Either make `LedgerWriter::commit` return a commit `NodeId`/commit result, or explicitly defer `head_t` mutation and remove the “CO1.7 owns head_t now” claim from spec §0/§3/§5.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5549:handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:98:17. Add 2 new test stubs (signature binding + transplant defense) to lift count from 6 → 8
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5563:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:29:-   **Implementation confidence**: The new test case `signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense` significantly increases confidence. It exercises the real Ed25519 sign/verify path through the new typed emitter, confirming the mechanism works end-to-end. Crucially, it also asserts that tampering with a signed field (`parent_ledger_root`) correctly invalidates the signature, proving the K2 transplant defense is effective in code. This lifts implementation confidence to the level required for a PASS.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5564:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:32:v1.2 still warrants a PASS. The changes from v1.1 are all positive regressions or fixes. The patches implement a design I previously approved, tighten the atom's scope, and fix minor inconsistencies. The new test provides strong, concrete evidence that the security-critical signing path is correctly implemented. No aspect of the design that earned my round-2 PASS has been compromised or weakened.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5566:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:112:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.

handover/audits/GEMINI_WHITEPAPER_V2_AUDIT_2026-04-27_R3.md:62:- **Subsumption**: The appendix explicitly maps `§ 5.1 Layer 4 Transition Ledger` to the `CO1.7 transition_ledger` atom, confirming it correctly subsumes the planned work.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:58:**Q8. PASS gate**: does v1.2 close all R2 findings cleanly enough for CO1.7-impl A2 unblock?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:85:**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:119:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:127:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:131:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:154:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:155:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:156:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:157:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:158:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:167:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:169:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:172:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:173:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:252:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:277:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:285:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:295:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:327:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:333:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:

handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:24:PASS. The commitment in spec § 0.1 is strong and sufficient to close the round-1 constitutional violation on cold replay (Art 0.2). The language "CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) **MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**" is a binding, machine-checkable gate. The spec further reinforces this by stating it is a "necessary condition for CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS".
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:26:This pattern of documenting explicit, hard cross-atom ordering dependencies is consistent with project precedent for managing complex, multi-atom features (e.g., the deferrals within the CO1.7 spec itself). It honestly represents the technical dependency without blocking parallel work on unrelated implementation stages (A2, A3). This fully closes my top must-fix from round-1.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:50:The enforcement of Inv 3 (escrow conservation) belongs to the transition function bodies (CO1.7.5 atom) and their associated conformance tests, which would need to test multiple escrow-balance scenarios. For this ABI-defining atom, a single fixture is sufficient to lock the wire format.

handover/audits/CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:24:| **C-1** | `replay_full_transition` accepts only `genesis_state_root` + `genesis_ledger_root`; fabricates `QState::genesis()` and patches roots. **Drops budget / registries / balances / task_markets** — CO1.7.5 cannot reconstruct real state from this API | transition_ledger.rs:348+368 | Real defect |
handover/audits/CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:33:| **G-1** | `head_t` constitutional alignment (Art 0.4) — Sequencer never mutates head_t; QState::default empty string for entire CO1.7-impl runtime | Q4 | Carry-forward to CO1.7.5 (per K3 v1.2 deferred-by-design) |
handover/audits/CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:61:| **G-1** head_t Art 0.4 | DEFERRED to CO1.7.5 | Per CO1.7 spec K3 v1.2 (already PASS/PASS audited): head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring when Git2LedgerWriter exposes commit_sha. Both auditors agree the spec-level deferral is acceptable for this gate; closure happens in CO1.7.5 atom. **Documented as a CO1.7.5 hard prerequisite** in the next atom's spec. |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:142:### 0.3 Two STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3 supersessions adopted by CO1.7.5
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:144:CO1.7.5 inherits two prior minimization decisions that diverge from STATE § 3 v1.4 pseudocode. Both were ratified by downstream specs reaching dual-audit PASS/PASS; CO1.7.5 carries them forward unchanged. Re-audit gating for the STATE spec itself is **not** decided here — CO1.7.5 spec only documents the carry-forward; whether STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC needs a v1.5 housekeeping commit is a separate decision for the STATE spec curator.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:149:| STATE § 3 line 412 (and parallel lines in § 3.1 line 467, § 3.2 line 561) | `q_next.head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(new_state_root)` inside the pure transition body | STATE v1.4 round-4 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:150:| CO1.7 K3 v1.2 § 5 | "`NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version"; head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ Sequencer post-commit | CO1.7 v1.2 round-3 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:152:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies **never** mutate `q_next.head_t`. Pure function returns `q_next` with `head_t == q.head_t`. `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` happens exclusively in `Sequencer::apply_one` post-commit (§ 1 D2 below). All 7 transition bodies inherit this rule.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:157:| STATE § 3 lines 403-409 (and parallels in § 3.1 / § 3.2) | `SignalBundle { boolean: vec![Signal::Boolean(BoolSignal::AcceptedAt(...))], statistical: vec![Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::PriceUpdate(...)), Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::ReputationDelta(...))] }` (two-axis vec with Bool/Stat sub-variants) | STATE v1.4 round-4 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:158:| CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2 + shipped `src/state/typed_tx.rs:830-854` | `SignalBundle { kind: SignalKind }` with 4 variants only: `Empty / Finalize { claim_id, reward } / TaskExpired { task_id, bounty_refunded } / TerminalSummary { run_id, outcome }` | CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:160:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies emit `SignalBundle` matching the **shipped** 4-variant SignalKind only. Per the CO1.1.4-pre1 doc-comment "Full L6 signal-stream design is CO1.9", the BoolSignal/StatSignal richness is deferred. Shipped emit table:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:172:CO1.9 will extend SignalKind with the deferred reputation / price-update / acceptance-event variants; CO1.7.5 makes **zero** SignalKind additions (preserves CO1.1.4-pre1 ABI lock).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:174:### 0.4 Pre-implementation gate (from CO1.7 § 12)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:175:> "CO1.7 must reach PASS/PASS before implementing CO1.7.5 (transition function bodies) + CO1.4-extra (CAS persistence)."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:177:CO1.7-impl reached PASS/PASS-equivalent at commit `2461fe6` (per LATEST.md 2026-04-28 session-2 summary). CO1.4-extra shipped at commit `b6b7574`. **Pre-implementation gate is therefore satisfied**; CO1.7.5 spec is unblocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:208:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:218:// NEW (CO1.7.5 D2): head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:224:**Atomicity** (extends v1.1 C-2 closure): under the acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and two field assignments (infallible). There is no failure point between commit success and `head_t` advancement; `q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` therefore advance atomically with `next_logical_t`. The single-writer per (runtime_repo, run_id) invariant from CO1.7 § 5.2.1 is preserved.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:226:**Required trait extension**: the existing `LedgerWriter` trait does **NOT** expose `head_commit_oid()` (only `Git2LedgerWriter` has it). CO1.7.5 adds:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:234:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5): canonical 40-char lowercase hex commit OID of the most
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:251:**Why `if let Some(...)` instead of `unwrap()`**: tolerates `InMemoryLedgerWriter` deterministically. CO1.7.5+ never panics on `None`; it simply leaves `head_t` stale, which is acceptable for unit tests (they assert on `ledger_root_t` only, per the CO1.7-impl test suite).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:255:`src/bus.rs:9` and `src/kernel.rs:8` both use legacy `crate::ledger::{Ledger, Node, NodeId, EventType, TapeError}` (retirement is CO1.1.5's job, not CO1.7.5's). `Bus::new(kernel: Kernel, config: BusConfig)` (bus.rs:87) shows Bus owns Kernel; the architecture below exploits this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:263:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5 D3): None when kernel runs in legacy-only mode.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:305:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:509:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |

Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
