handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:541:### In scope (CO1.7 atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:547:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:548:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:554:- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:555:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:560:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:563:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:692:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:734:├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:739:├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:740:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:743:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:767:    /// **C3**: signing key handle (CO1.7.0a-f).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:771:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:863:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:875:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:900:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:915:/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:946:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:964:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:968:**Implementation (CO1.7.5+)**: `Git2LedgerWriter` (built on existing CO1.4 CAS); skeleton `InMemoryLedgerWriter` for v1 testing.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1102:  - CO1.7.5 (transition function bodies): separate downstream atom
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1129:| `cargo check` on `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` | PASS | LedgerEntry / TxKind / append / replay_chain_integrity / InMemoryLedgerWriter all type-check against shipped CO1.4 + CO1.7.0a-f + Q_t types |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1147:| K3 L4/L5 head_t ownership | Boundary clarified: CO1.7 owns ledger_root + commit-chain head_t (NodeId(commit_sha)); CO1.8 owns state_root | § 0, § 3, § 5 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1151:| K7 conformance test gap | Explicit 8 tests (4 skeleton + 4 CO1.7.5+ stage) | § 7 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1162:# XREF: CO1.1.4-pre1 spec (frozen, PASS/PASS) — supplies ABI lock authority
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1168:**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1202:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1210:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1214:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1237:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1238:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1239:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1240:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1241:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1250:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1252:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1255:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1256:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1335:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1360:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1368:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1378:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1410:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1416:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1500:| 4+ | … | … | … | iterate to PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1502:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1504:**Audit cost estimate**: ~$15-25 (smaller surface than CO1.7 spec @ $25-42; mostly type definitions + 2 plausibly-derived schemas).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1513:- **Total atom budget**: ~1.5-2.5 days from spec draft to PASS/PASS.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1519:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1521:3. **Versioning**: `extensions: BTreeMap<String, Vec<u8>>` is on `LedgerEntry` (CO1.7); per-tx forward compat is via additive variants on `TypedTx` (e.g. `TypedTx::MetaTx(...)` lands in v4.1). No per-struct `version` field.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1856:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7853:## Why fix now (not post-PASS/PASS)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7855:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8145:   101	In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8155:   111	// NEW (CO1.7.5 D2): head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8161:   117	**Atomicity** (extends v1.1 C-2 closure): under the acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and two field assignments (infallible). There is no failure point between commit success and `head_t` advancement; `q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` therefore advance atomically with `next_logical_t`. The single-writer per (runtime_repo, run_id) invariant from CO1.7 § 5.2.1 is preserved.

handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:18:-   **Art 0.4 Q_t version-controlled state:** The structure is correct. `LedgerEntry` (CO1.7) is the version-control envelope, akin to a git commit object, containing metadata and a pointer (`tx_payload_cid`) to the content. `TypedTx` is the content itself, akin to a git blob. This cleanly separates the transport/envelope layer from the payload/content layer, a robust architectural pattern.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:50:-   **CRITICAL FLAW:** The spec for CO1.7 (§0) and this atom's context correctly identify that if CAS bytes are lost, the system enters a state of partial verifiability. The L4 `LedgerEntry` chain can be verified (signatures over CIDs are intact), but the state transitions cannot be replayed, as the payloads are gone. This **violates Art 0.2 (Tape Canonical 公理)**, which demands that "all signals must be reconstructible from the tape." A tape of pointers to missing data is not sufficient for reconstruction. While the fix (CAS index persistence) belongs to CO1.4-extra, this atom cannot PASS without a concrete, committed, and immediate plan to resolve this constitutional violation. The current state introduces a dangerous "trust mode" ambiguity.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:70:-   **CRITICAL FLAW:** The `system_signature` field is highly suspect. The `LedgerEntry` (CO1.7) envelope is already signed by the system. This signature covers the `tx_payload_cid`, which is the digest of the `FinalizeRewardTx`. A second system signature inside the payload appears redundant. If it signs something different (e.g., a message from a separate SettlementEngine service), the spec **must** define the canonical pre-image for this signature. As it stands, this is a dangerous ambiguity. The field should likely be **DROPPED**.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:74:**Verdict: CHALLENGE.** The use of identical serde adapters for `AgentSignature` and `SystemSignature` creates a type confusion vulnerability at the wire level. The domain separation in the CO1.7 envelope is necessary but not sufficient.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:80:-   CO1.7's `LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` correctly uses a domain separator (`b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"`). This protects the *envelope*. However, the `AgentSignature` in a `WorkTx` signs the canonical digest of the `WorkTx` payload itself. The spec and implementation **do not specify a domain separator** for the agent-signed payloads. This is a critical omission. Every signed payload type (`WorkTx`, `VerifyTx`, `ChallengeTx`) must have its own unique domain separator in its `canonical_digest` pre-image (e.g., `b"turingosv4.work_tx.v1"`).

Codex reviews that doc patch before code.

handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND4_2026-04-26.md:3342:### F-2026-04-25-01: Paper 1 v2.1 round-3 dual-audit PASS/PASS — arXiv-ready
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:82:### Q9. The retry-metadata system-stamping (CO1.7.0 + CO1.7.0b) introduces a new system-signing keypair. Where is this keypair generated, where is it stored, who can rotate it, and what stops a compromised system instance from forging fake retry metadata?
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:91:This is not a minor detail; it is a fundamental security design flaw in the plan. The atoms `CO1.7.0` and `CO1.7.0b` cannot proceed until a full specification for the system keypair's lifecycle and security is written, audited, and approved.
handover/audits/GEMINI_V32_REVIEW_2026-04-27.md:125:2.  **[VETO] Define System Keypair Security (Q9)**: The plan must be amended with a full security specification for the new system-signing keypair, covering generation, storage, rotation, and the compromise threat model. This is a prerequisite for atoms CO1.7.0 and CO1.7.0b.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:101:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:106:**Round-1 verdicts**: `handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md` + `handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md`; merged in `handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:109:- `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` v1.4 — typed schemas + step_transition pseudocode + 27 invariants (round-4 PASS/PASS)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:110:- `SYSTEM_KEYPAIR_SECURITY_v1_2026-04-27.md` — runtime keypair lifecycle (CO1.7.0a-f, done @ Wave 4-B)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:122:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:123:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:135:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:140:| K3 | L4/L5 head_t ownership inconsistent (spec line 194 vs 276 disagreed) | CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + commit-chain `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` only; L5 (CO1.8) owns `state_root_t` mutation; sequencer drops `head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(...)` line | Codex Q-E |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:141:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:144:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:154:### In scope (CO1.7 atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:160:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:161:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:167:- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:168:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:173:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:176:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:305:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:347:├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:352:├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:353:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:356:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:380:    /// **C3**: signing key handle (CO1.7.0a-f).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:384:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:476:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:488:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:513:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:528:/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:559:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:577:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:581:**Implementation (CO1.7.5+)**: `Git2LedgerWriter` (built on existing CO1.4 CAS); skeleton `InMemoryLedgerWriter` for v1 testing.

It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:660:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:662:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2410:### In scope (CO1.7 atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2416:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2423:- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2424:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + `head_t`; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2429:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2432:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2561:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2593:├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2598:├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2599:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2602:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2720:        // path is CO1.7.5+ wiring concern.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2729:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` and the commit-chain `head_t`; CO1.8 (L5 materializer) owns `state_root_t` mutation. Sequencer accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as the transition function returns it; sequencer does NOT call `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3975:CO1.7 publishes a single function `dispatch_transition(q, typed_tx) -> (q_next, signals)` that the sequencer's `apply_one` calls. Existing transition functions in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3-3.7` are wired into this dispatch:

CO1.7 itself only ships the dispatch + sequencer + ledger writer; the transition function bodies are stubs (`unimplemented!()`) that downstream atoms fill.

It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wal.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required**. The progression from Layer 0 (Constitution Root) to Layer 6 (Signal Indices) follows a clear dependency path from trust anchor to derived analytics. There is no significant overlap between layers. Layer 4 (Transition Ledger) correctly serves as the append-only source of truth from which Layer 5 (Materialized State) and Layer 6 are derived. The appendix (`附录 A`) confirms that Layer 4 is intended to subsume the `CO1.7 transition_ledger` atom.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10446:     8	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC2-Append + WP § 5.L4: L4 transition ledger (CO1.7 type skeleton).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10468:# Codex CO1.7 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10473:DIV-2 real but overstated as an L4 blocker. `QState` economic fields are currently BTreeMap newtype shells, not domain mutation APIs ([q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:143), [q_state.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/q_state.rs:157)). CO1.7 can still implement ledger append/storage and chain replay; full economic transition bodies remain blocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10498:Partial replay is acceptable only as skeleton smoke, not as a v1 CO1.7 deliverable. Spec says replay is the I-DETHASH witness at lines 219-264 and invariant witness at lines 305-306. Skeleton explicitly does chain-only replay and does not verify signatures, payload recovery, or transition recomputation ([transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:240), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:270)).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10505:CO1.7 can own ledger roots and commit-chain heads. L5 must own state_root materialization. Spec v1.1 should define a stub `StateRootProvider`/materializer boundary or defer `head_t` mutation out of CO1.7.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10524:CAS cold replay is not actually supported by shipped `CasStore`: `open()` initializes an empty in-memory index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:67)), and `get()` requires that index ([store.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs:111)). CO1.7 replay cannot recover payloads after restart unless CAS metadata is persisted or ledger commits include enough blob lookup data.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10532:Conformance gap is large: spec promises 8 CO1.7 tests lines 318-331; skeleton has 6 inline unit tests and none for signature, CAS recovery, sequencer serialization, full replay, or canonical fixtures.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10553:# Gemini CO1.7 transition_ledger Round-1 Audit Run
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10560:# Gemini CO1.7 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10564:The CO1.7 design demonstrates strong alignment with the TuringOS Constitution and the Anti-Oreo architecture.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10585:-   **Preserve WP § 5.L4 axioms?**: **PASS**. The whitepaper defines the logical content of a `WorkTx`. The CO1.7 `LedgerEntry` is an envelope that contains a content-addressed identifier (`tx_payload_cid`) pointing to the canonical `WorkTx` payload. All 12 fields specified in WP § 5.L4 are preserved within the CAS-stored payload. The envelope adds sequencer-level metadata (`logical_t`, `system_signature`, etc.), which is a correct separation of concerns between agent-proposed state and system-accepted state.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10597:-   **A partial Art 0.2 implementation that should NOT be called "I-DETHASH witness" until full?**: **CHALLENGE**. Correct. Calling the current chain-integrity check a full "I-DETHASH witness" is an overstatement. It is a witness for *chain integrity*, not for *deterministic state transition*. The name should be qualified, for example, as an "I-DETHASH chain-integrity witness," until the full replay (spec § 4 `replay` function, including re-running pure transitions) is implemented in CO1.7.5+.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10621:The CO1.7 design is neutral with respect to the final resolution of INV8 (DAG determinism).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10623:-   The core of the CO1.7 design is the `Sequencer`, which imposes a **strict, linear, total ordering** on all incoming transactions (spec § 3, § 5.2.1). It serializes concurrent submissions into a single, unambiguous sequence based on `logical_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10672:The CO1.7 design is constitutionally aligned and architecturally sound in its core principles. However, the audit has identified several significant strategic risks, ambiguities, and integration failures that must be resolved before implementation proceeds beyond the skeleton. The design prioritizes v1 simplicity at the cost of creating a temporary trust model ambiguity (Q3) and lacks explicit forward-compatibility affordances (Q7, Q9). The indirection to CAS (Q2) creates an availability dependency that must be managed. The integration with the existing signing primitive (Q4) is unresolved.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10693:# CO1.7 Round-1 Dual External Audit — Merged Verdict

Spec v1.1 explicit boundary; defer `head_t` mutation OR define `StateRootProvider` stub. |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10836:> - **§ 2.5 + § 7 fixture corpus defer-ack** (Q5/NEW-5): canonical serialization RULE frozen v1.4; full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzz seed lands in CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7 atoms (not v1.x spec scope).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11151:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14261:   532	Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14263:   534	CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14382:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:43:- **LedgerEntrySigningPayload**: the 8-field bytes-on-the-wire that the system keypair actually signs (distinct from LedgerEntry-the-stored-record).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14788:- **Q-A6 K3 L4/L5 boundary: PARTIAL.** `from_state_root` is removed, but §3 still says `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` while `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash`, not commit SHA; see spec [§3](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:347) and [§5](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:438).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14789:- **Q-A7 K4 trait: CLOSED.** `commit(&mut self) -> Hash` matches skeleton; `iter_from` deferral is OK for CO1.7.5+.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14797:- **Q-B1: NEW-ISSUE.** The CAS metadata timing is not racy under the stated single-driver sequencer, but spec §3 uses `ObjectType::Transition` at [line 295](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:293); shipped `ObjectType` has no such variant in [schema.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/schema.rs:42). Epoch in `creator` is not a material leak because epoch is public in the entry.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:14813:2. **Fix the K3 head_t/commit return contradiction.** Either make `LedgerWriter::commit` return a commit `NodeId`/commit result, or explicitly defer `head_t` mutation and remove the “CO1.7 owns head_t now” claim from spec §0/§3/§5.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15054:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:1:# CO1.7 Transition Ledger v1.2 — Round-2 closure
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15055:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:3:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15056:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:20:## v1.2 patch log (vs. v1.1) — round-2 closure
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15057:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:22:| ID | v1.1 issue | v1.2 fix | Source |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15058:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:24:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15059:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:25:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15060:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:26:| **R2-C2-CAS** | Spec § 3 sequencer pseudocode used `ObjectType::Transition` but shipped `ObjectType` has no such variant | Spec § 3 changed to `ObjectType::ProposalPayload` (the existing variant for agent work_tx payloads — semantically correct, no schema extension needed) | Codex round-2 must-fix #3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15061:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:27:| **R2-typo** | Spec § 1.1 said "8-field bytes-on-the-wire" but `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` actually has 9 fields | Updated to "9-field" | Codex Q-C2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15062:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:39:| C3 | signing primitive integration via `CanonicalMessage` enum was unspecified; spec called nonexistent digest-form verifier | Path A: extend `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(LedgerEntrySigningPayload)`; sign separate signing payload (NOT raw `LedgerEntry`); new API `keypair.sign_ledger_entry(payload) → SystemSignature` | Codex Q-G + Gemini Q4 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15063:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:41:| K2 | signature did NOT bind `parent_ledger_root` → transplant attack | `LedgerEntrySigningPayload` includes `parent_ledger_root` field | Codex Q-B (NEW) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15064:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:42:| K3 | L4/L5 head_t ownership inconsistent (spec line 194 vs 276 disagreed) | CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + commit-chain `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` only; L5 (CO1.8) owns `state_root_t` mutation; sequencer drops `head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(...)` line | Codex Q-E |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15065:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:48:| D1 | epoch binding disagreement (Codex bind YES; Gemini bind NO) | Conservative resolution: epoch IS bound in `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`; epoch NOT separately folded into `ledger_root_t` (Codex security wins; Gemini orthogonality preserved at the ledger_root axis) | merged verdict § 5 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15066:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:58:- **LedgerEntrySigningPayload**: the 9-field bytes-on-the-wire that the system keypair actually signs (distinct from LedgerEntry-the-stored-record).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15067:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:63:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15068:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:64:- **CanonicalMessage extension**: extends shipped `CanonicalMessage` enum with `LedgerEntrySigning([u8; 32])` opaque-digest variant (R2 design — avoids circular module dep); new typed sign API `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry(keypair, signing_payload_digest)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15069:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:70:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15070:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:120:    /// where signing_digest_t = canonical_digest(LedgerEntrySigningPayload at logical_t).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15074:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:179:impl LedgerEntrySigningPayload {
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15075:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:205:### § 1.2 CanonicalMessage extension (per C3; **shipped in v1.2**)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15076:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:207:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15077:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:210:// In src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs (additive Wave 4-B extension; SHIPPED v1.2):

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15098:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:381:        // and chain-integrity tests do NOT depend on head_t.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15099:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:390:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15100:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:461:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15978:    78	- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16107:   207	CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16149:   249	├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16154:   254	├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16155:   255	└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16158:   258	**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16182:   282	    /// **C3**: signing key handle (CO1.7.0a-f).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16186:   286	    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16278:   378	        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16290:   390	**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16315:   415	    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16330:   430	/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16361:   461	- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:16376:/bin/bash -lc "sha256sum handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs src/bottom_white/ledger/mod.rs genesis_payload.toml && rg -n \"CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER|src/bottom_white/ledger/(system_keypair|transition_ledger|mod)\\.rs\" genesis_payload.toml" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4

handover/audits/CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_FINAL_2026-04-28.md:96:7. **Codex-only narrow rounds are efficient closure mechanism**: when Gemini has already accepted the design and only patch-mechanical defects remain, dispatch Codex-only saves ~$3-5/round. CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7-impl bundle both used this pattern successfully.

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:1467:The constitutional FC3-S3 `readonly` subgraph contains FC3-N3 (`constitution as ground truth`) and FC3-N4 (`logs archive as ground truth`). PREREG § 1.8 (round-4 dual-audit PASS/PASS) extended this base for the PPUT-CCL experiment. Each addition is a research-protocol orphan with explicit constitutional justification (case-law / measurement-fidelity / pre-registration commitment).
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5812:./handover/ai-direct/LATEST.md:3:**Session Summary**: B7 (Trust Root + Boot freeze) → 用户 atomic-alignment critique (3 flowcharts) → B7 alignment fix → B7-extra rollback toggle + calibration scripts → dual audit round-1 VETO/VETO → 13-fix landing → simplifier pass → **constitution amendment (sudo)**: V.1.1 sudo scope + V.1.2 ArchitectAI commit authority + V.1.3 JudgeAI→Veto-AI + V.3 amendment log → re-audit round 2 VETO/PASS (Codex caught self-inflicted regression: Q7.b silently absorbed TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panics) → round-2 fix → re-audit round 3 CHALLENGE/CHALLENGE (problem_file_missing absorption + boot preflight `||true` exit-discard + EXIT=0+empty PPUT_RESULT non-exhaustive) → round-3 fix (commit `d0d474e`) → **round-4 audit in flight**. **187/187 cargo test PASS** + 20 ignored. Trust Root manifest **20 files** (was 15 — added main.rs / Cargo.lock / runner.sh / compute_p0.py per audit). User authorized auto-research overnight to PROCEED on PASS/PASS.

Sedimented: when claiming "row removed", verify with grep on the spec doc — claim-vs-doc parity has been an audit finding before (CO1.7 round-2 R2-C3 was the same pattern: spec said "C3 CLOSED" while code had no LedgerEntry path).

handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:672:> § 2. Amendment scope: extend the 30-day arc clock by the wall-clock duration of TFR (sprints S0 through S6.4, capped at **10 weeks = 70 days**). Phase A5 commit gate's "30 days from PASS/PASS" timer pauses at TFR commit S0.7 (C2 batch FROZEN file landing) and resumes at TFR commit S6.5 (arc-clock-restart governance landing). All Phase A through Phase D work performed before TFR remains valid (no re-do); Phase C C2 batch results pre-TFR are preserved (`discarded_12way_run_2026-04-24/`) but not used for inferential decisions; the post-TFR re-run (S6.4) produces the canonical Phase C C2 dataset.
handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:674:> § 3. Pause-condition explicit: any TFR sprint dual-audit VETO halts both the TFR clock and the arc clock. Resumption requires VETO-fix-then-proceed dual-audit PASS/PASS.
handover/architect-insights/TFR_MASTER_PLAN_2026-04-26.md:810:S0 sprint exit ceremony: dual external audit on (S0.1, S0.2, S0.4 spike outcome, S0.5) → PASS/PASS gates S1.

handover/architect-insights/CO_MEGA_PLAN_v3.1_2026-04-26.md:271:| **CO2.4.0** (NEW) | **DESIGN SPIKE**: specify provably deterministic Contribution DAG **CONSTRUCTION** algorithm (not just weight function — Codex CO P0.7 audit Q3 flagged that "same DAG → same weights" tests the easy half; the hard half is "same inputs → same DAG"). Inputs = L4 read_set/write_set tuples (deterministic) + Tx parent state roots + chronological tx ordering by (timestamp, tx_id) tiebreak. Outputs = canonical DAG (same inputs → byte-identical adjacency list AND byte-identical weights). Must address: (a) concurrent write_tx ordering, (b) multi-parent merge ambiguity, (c) citation transitivity, (d) edge type discrimination (builds-on vs cites vs reuses) from L4 fields ONLY (no agent self-declaration), (e) cycle detection (DAG must be acyclic). **Deliverable**: 1-page algorithm spec + 3-tx adversarial worked example with byte-identical expected output for two independent runs. Dual audit gate (Codex + Gemini PASS/PASS) before CO2.4.1 starts. | `handover/architect-insights/INV8_DAG_DETERMINISM_SPEC_2026-04-26.md` (drafted in CO P2.4 entry) |

handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:153:### F-2026-04-25-01: Paper 1 v2.1 round-3 dual-audit PASS/PASS — arXiv-ready
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:27:**Why this atom exists**: when CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types per STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1 are required but **none of them existed in code** (only `MicroCoin`). Per (b) decision (atom split, not absorption): CO1.1.4-pre1 ships the ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, before A2-A4 unblock.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:41:- **PASS** unblocks CO1.7-impl A2 (TypedTx + transition stubs → Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition); and is a transitive dependency of CO P2.x economy atoms (TaskId / AgentSignature / etc. are reused).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:63:**Q-D. Bincode v2 settings & cross-platform stability**: CO1.7-impl A1 (commit `a03cc52`) froze `bincode_canonical_config()` = `standard().with_big_endian().with_fixed_int_encoding()`. The CO1.1.4-pre1 tests reuse this codec via `canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode`. Concerns:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:72:**Q-F. SignalBundle minimal v1 — does it actually work for CO1.7.5?**: spec § 0 says "minimal SignalBundle: a single enum-like discriminator + payload sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile". Implementation: `pub struct SignalBundle { pub kind: SignalKind }` with 4 variants (Empty/Finalize/TaskExpired/TerminalSummary).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:73:- Does CO1.7.5 step_transition / verify_transition / challenge_transition / reuse_transition need additional variants this v1 didn't anticipate?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:78:- Is the `NotYetImplemented` sentinel a code smell that audit should flag? (It's the explicit interface CO1.7.5 will fill in; documented in spec § 0.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:122:**Status**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:124:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:128:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:151:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:152:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:153:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:154:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:155:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:236:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:264:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:296:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:302:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3757:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3778:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |

Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3791:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3796:| K3 | L4/L5 head_t ownership inconsistent (spec line 194 vs 276 disagreed) | CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + commit-chain `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` only; L5 (CO1.8) owns `state_root_t` mutation; sequencer drops `head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(...)` line | Codex Q-E |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3797:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3800:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3810:### In scope (CO1.7 atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3816:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3817:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3823:- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3824:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3829:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3832:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3961:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4003:├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4008:├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4009:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4012:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4036:    /// **C3**: signing key handle (CO1.7.0a-f).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4040:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4132:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4144:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4169:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4184:/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4215:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4233:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4237:**Implementation (CO1.7.5+)**: `Git2LedgerWriter` (built on existing CO1.4 CAS); skeleton `InMemoryLedgerWriter` for v1 testing.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4277:**v1 stage (skeleton)**: 8 tests (6 already in skeleton + 2 NEW K2/Q9). **CO1.7.5+ stage**: 4 more.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4309:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4316:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4318:CO1.7 also extends `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs` (CanonicalMessage variant + sign API); that file is NOT STEP_B-restricted and the change is additive (no behavior change to existing 3 variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4342:| Q6 replay error mode | open | **Reject on first error** (current). Diagnostic-collection mode is a v4.x extension; first-error simplicity matches CO1.7.5 implementation budget. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4347:| Q11 NEW open Qs | — | Codex round-1 listed: parent_ledger_root binding (now K2 / done), rejected-submission logical time (now K1 / done), CAS persistence (now C2 → CO1.4-extra), canonical fixtures (deferred to CO1.7.5+ test stubs), L4/L5 head_t ownership (now K3 / done). All addressed. |
