handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:30:1. **D1 — 7 per-kind transition function bodies** translating `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3 / § 3.1-3.4 / § 3.6 / § 3.7` pseudocode into deterministic pure Rust, with two CO1.7-K3-v1.2 / CO1.1.4-pre1 supersessions carried forward (§ 0.3).
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:33:4. **D4 — Un-ignore `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity`** + 3 NEW CO1.7.5+ stage tests already declared in `CO1_7 § 7` table.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:35:### 0.3 Two STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3 supersessions adopted by CO1.7.5
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:37:CO1.7.5 inherits two prior minimization decisions that diverge from STATE § 3 v1.4 pseudocode. Both were ratified by downstream specs reaching dual-audit PASS/PASS; CO1.7.5 carries them forward unchanged. Re-audit gating for the STATE spec itself is **not** decided here — CO1.7.5 spec only documents the carry-forward; whether STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC needs a v1.5 housekeeping commit is a separate decision for the STATE spec curator.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:43:| CO1.7 K3 v1.2 § 5 | "`NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version"; head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ Sequencer post-commit | CO1.7 v1.2 round-3 PASS/PASS |
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:45:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies **never** mutate `q_next.head_t`. Pure function returns `q_next` with `head_t == q.head_t`. `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` happens exclusively in `Sequencer::apply_one` post-commit (§ 1 D2 below). All 7 transition bodies inherit this rule.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:51:| CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2 + shipped `src/state/typed_tx.rs:830-854` | `SignalBundle { kind: SignalKind }` with 4 variants only: `Empty / Finalize { claim_id, reward } / TaskExpired { task_id, bounty_refunded } / TerminalSummary { run_id, outcome }` | CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS/PASS |
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:53:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies emit `SignalBundle` matching the **shipped** 4-variant SignalKind only. Per the CO1.1.4-pre1 doc-comment "Full L6 signal-stream design is CO1.9", the BoolSignal/StatSignal richness is deferred. Shipped emit table:
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:65:CO1.9 will extend SignalKind with the deferred reputation / price-update / acceptance-event variants; CO1.7.5 makes **zero** SignalKind additions (preserves CO1.1.4-pre1 ABI lock).
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:68:> "CO1.7 must reach PASS/PASS before implementing CO1.7.5 (transition function bodies) + CO1.4-extra (CAS persistence)."
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:70:CO1.7-impl reached PASS/PASS-equivalent at commit `2461fe6` (per LATEST.md 2026-04-28 session-2 summary). CO1.4-extra shipped at commit `b6b7574`. **Pre-implementation gate is therefore satisfied**; CO1.7.5 spec is unblocked.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:90:**Total**: ~460-700 LoC in 7 transition body files. Each gets a unit test module covering happy path + each `TransitionError` variant the function can return (per CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2 closed-taxonomy invariant).
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:101:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:111:// NEW (CO1.7.5 D2): head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:119:**Required trait extension**: the existing `LedgerWriter` trait does **NOT** expose `head_commit_oid()` (only `Git2LedgerWriter` has it). CO1.7.5 adds:
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:127:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5): canonical 40-char lowercase hex commit OID of the most
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:144:**Why `if let Some(...)` instead of `unwrap()`**: tolerates `InMemoryLedgerWriter` deterministically. CO1.7.5+ never panics on `None`; it simply leaves `head_t` stale, which is acceptable for unit tests (they assert on `ledger_root_t` only, per the CO1.7-impl test suite).
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:148:`src/bus.rs:9` and `src/kernel.rs:8` both use legacy `crate::ledger::{Ledger, Node, NodeId, EventType, TapeError}` (retirement is CO1.1.5's job, not CO1.7.5's). `Bus::new(kernel: Kernel, config: BusConfig)` (bus.rs:87) shows Bus owns Kernel; the architecture below exploits this.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:156:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5 D3): None when kernel runs in legacy-only mode.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:198:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:200:### D4 — 4 CO1.7.5+ stage conformance tests + un-ignore
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:208:| `tests/cas_payload_round_trip` | NEW | `CasStore::put` → `CasStore::get` round-trip; CID stable across runs (post-CO1.4-extra cold-restart). |
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:209:| `tests/sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` | un-ignore (currently `#[ignore = "CO1.7.5: requires real per-kind transition bodies"]` at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:1451`) | Submit 100 TypedTx through Sequencer; replay → byte-identical `state_root`. |
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:217:1. **Legacy `src/ledger.rs` retirement** — CO1.1.5 atom (per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`). CO1.7.5 leaves the legacy WAL ledger fully running; bus.rs / kernel.rs continue to call `self.ledger.append(...)` for non-typed events.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:220:4. **Submission queue back-pressure tuning** — Q2 fixed at deterministic exponential backoff (Q2 v1.1 resolution); CO1.7.5 inherits it. Per-task fairness deferred to v4.x.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:221:5. **STATE § 3 line 412 NodeId::from_state_root patch** — CO1.7.5 spec lands D1-D4. STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC v1.5 single-line clarification is a separate housekeeping commit (NOT re-audit; § 0.3 above).
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:222:6. **Materializer state_root computation** — CO1.8 atom (L5). CO1.7.5 transition bodies build `q_next.state_root_t` via the same `q_next.economic_state_t.derive_state_root()` accessor that CO1.7-impl tests already exercise; CO1.8 will replace this with a real merkleized materializer.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:236:| **Q4 SignalBundle population** | **CLOSED**: shipped 4-variant `SignalKind` (Empty/Finalize/TaskExpired/TerminalSummary) suffices for all 7 transition bodies; full emit table in § 0.3.2 above. | Smoke read of `src/state/typed_tx.rs:830-854` confirmed CO1.1.4-pre1 minimization. STATE § 3 BoolSignal/StatSignal richness is CO1.9 scope; CO1.7.5 makes zero SignalKind additions. |
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:260:Coverage: 22 / 22 shipped variants accounted for (15 used + 7 indirectly: `NotYetImplemented` is the stub sentinel that disappears post-CO1.7.5; the others map 1-to-1). CO1.7.5 adds **zero** new variants.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:288:- **Spec rounds**: 1-2 expected (CO1.7.5 inherits frozen ABI; 5 of 6 v1 open Qs already closed by smoke; only Q1 remains as audit input — substantively smaller surface than CO1.7 v1's 11 must-fix items).
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:294:- **Total atom budget**: ~1,000-1,560 LoC (down from initial 1,200-1,900 because Bus stays struct-shape-compatible after D3 simplification). **Estimated calendar time**: 4-7 days. Implementation may bundle as `CO1.7.5-impl A1+A2+A3+A4` like CO1.7-impl, OR ship as 4 sequential commits.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:301:1. **Two STATE § 3 supersessions adopted, not introduced** (§ 0.3): NodeId-from-commit-OID + 4-variant SignalKind. Both are CO1.7-K3-v1.2 and CO1.1.4-pre1 minimization decisions that already cleared dual audit; CO1.7.5 carries them forward unchanged. Auditors should validate the authority chain (CO1.7 v1.2 → CO1.7.5 vs STATE v1.4) — that's the substantive review surface, not the resolutions themselves.
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:306:6. **FC-trace requirements** (per CLAUDE.md "Alignment Standard"): every NEW pub symbol introduced by CO1.7.5 must carry a doc-comment `/// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role>` backlink. The set is: 7 transition body fns (each → STATE § 3.x); `LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid_hex` (→ § 5 L4 sequencer post-commit head_t wiring); `Kernel.sequencer` field + `Kernel::with_sequencer` + `Bus::submit_typed_tx` (→ § 5.2.1 single-writer entry-point). `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` witness-test additions are scoped under the implementation atom (not this spec).
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:340:| S2 | `#[ignore]` precedes `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` | `grep -B2 'fn sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity' src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` | `#[ignore = "CO1.7.5: requires real per-kind transition bodies"]` precedes `fn sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` | ✅ PASS (with reason string) |
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:359:- P5: § 0.3 restructured as **two** STATE supersessions (NodeId + SignalBundle); tightened "re-audit" framing (CO1.7.5 spec only documents carry-forward; STATE re-audit is the STATE curator's decision, not this spec's)
handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:372:3. then CO1.7.5-impl (D1 + D2 + D3 STEP_B + D4)

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:21:# Codex Round-3 Audit — CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.2 (narrow closure check)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:58:**Q8. PASS gate**: does v1.2 close all R2 findings cleanly enough for CO1.7-impl A2 unblock?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:62:# Codex CO1.1.4-pre1 Round-3 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:80:# CO1.1.4-pre1 spec v1.2 (target)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:81:# CO1.1.4-pre1 — Typed Tx ABI Surface (v1.2)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:119:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:120:| **P10** | TaskId-vs-TxId QState index mismatch (typed_tx uses TaskId; QState `task_markets_t` / `escrows_t` / `stakes_t` keyed by TxId) | This spec § 9 NEW D-4 documents the forward-migration plan: CO P2.1 (TaskMarket atom) owns the QState retrofit; v1.1 records the migration debt + cross-atom dependency note. Does NOT perform the retrofit (out of CO1.1.4-pre1 scope; would touch q_state.rs which is its own atom). | CX-3 (Codex Q-J) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:127:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:131:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:155:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:156:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:157:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:158:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:162:- `src/state/q_state.rs` (existing): keeps its existing types verbatim. CO1.1.4-pre1 only adds new types in `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:169:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:172:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:173:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:174:- CO1.4-extra has its own dual-audit gate.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:176:This ordering is a **necessary condition for CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS** per round-1 Gemini Q4; documented here so future audits cannot reinterpret silence as approval.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:277:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:333:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:401:| ID | STATE spec | CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 | Reason |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:406:| **D-4** (v1.1 NEW per Codex Q-J / CX-3) | QState `task_markets_t` / `escrows_t` / `stakes_t` keyed by `TxId` (q_state.rs:201/161/182) but typed_tx schemas use `TaskId` for the same task references | **NOT retrofit in this atom**. Migration owned by **CO P2.1 (TaskMarket atom)** which will rekey the QState indices to `TaskId`. CO1.1.4-pre1 documents the cross-atom debt; no wire-format consequence (the typed-tx schemas already use `TaskId` correctly per STATE § 1.2). |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:137:1. **D1 — 7 per-kind transition function bodies** translating `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3 / § 3.1-3.4 / § 3.6 / § 3.7` pseudocode into deterministic pure Rust, with two CO1.7-K3-v1.2 / CO1.1.4-pre1 supersessions carried forward (§ 0.3).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:140:4. **D4 — Un-ignore `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity`** + 3 NEW CO1.7.5+ stage tests already declared in `CO1_7 § 7` table.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:142:### 0.3 Two STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3 supersessions adopted by CO1.7.5
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:144:CO1.7.5 inherits two prior minimization decisions that diverge from STATE § 3 v1.4 pseudocode. Both were ratified by downstream specs reaching dual-audit PASS/PASS; CO1.7.5 carries them forward unchanged. Re-audit gating for the STATE spec itself is **not** decided here — CO1.7.5 spec only documents the carry-forward; whether STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC needs a v1.5 housekeeping commit is a separate decision for the STATE spec curator.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:150:| CO1.7 K3 v1.2 § 5 | "`NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version"; head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ Sequencer post-commit | CO1.7 v1.2 round-3 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:152:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies **never** mutate `q_next.head_t`. Pure function returns `q_next` with `head_t == q.head_t`. `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` happens exclusively in `Sequencer::apply_one` post-commit (§ 1 D2 below). All 7 transition bodies inherit this rule.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:158:| CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2 + shipped `src/state/typed_tx.rs:830-854` | `SignalBundle { kind: SignalKind }` with 4 variants only: `Empty / Finalize { claim_id, reward } / TaskExpired { task_id, bounty_refunded } / TerminalSummary { run_id, outcome }` | CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:160:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies emit `SignalBundle` matching the **shipped** 4-variant SignalKind only. Per the CO1.1.4-pre1 doc-comment "Full L6 signal-stream design is CO1.9", the BoolSignal/StatSignal richness is deferred. Shipped emit table:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:172:CO1.9 will extend SignalKind with the deferred reputation / price-update / acceptance-event variants; CO1.7.5 makes **zero** SignalKind additions (preserves CO1.1.4-pre1 ABI lock).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:175:> "CO1.7 must reach PASS/PASS before implementing CO1.7.5 (transition function bodies) + CO1.4-extra (CAS persistence)."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:177:CO1.7-impl reached PASS/PASS-equivalent at commit `2461fe6` (per LATEST.md 2026-04-28 session-2 summary). CO1.4-extra shipped at commit `b6b7574`. **Pre-implementation gate is therefore satisfied**; CO1.7.5 spec is unblocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:197:**Total**: ~460-700 LoC in 7 transition body files. Each gets a unit test module covering happy path + each `TransitionError` variant the function can return (per CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2 closed-taxonomy invariant).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:208:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:218:// NEW (CO1.7.5 D2): head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:226:**Required trait extension**: the existing `LedgerWriter` trait does **NOT** expose `head_commit_oid()` (only `Git2LedgerWriter` has it). CO1.7.5 adds:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:234:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5): canonical 40-char lowercase hex commit OID of the most
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:251:**Why `if let Some(...)` instead of `unwrap()`**: tolerates `InMemoryLedgerWriter` deterministically. CO1.7.5+ never panics on `None`; it simply leaves `head_t` stale, which is acceptable for unit tests (they assert on `ledger_root_t` only, per the CO1.7-impl test suite).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:255:`src/bus.rs:9` and `src/kernel.rs:8` both use legacy `crate::ledger::{Ledger, Node, NodeId, EventType, TapeError}` (retirement is CO1.1.5's job, not CO1.7.5's). `Bus::new(kernel: Kernel, config: BusConfig)` (bus.rs:87) shows Bus owns Kernel; the architecture below exploits this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:263:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5 D3): None when kernel runs in legacy-only mode.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:305:**`src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs` is NOT touched by CO1.7.5**. Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:307:### D4 — 4 CO1.7.5+ stage conformance tests + un-ignore
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:315:| `tests/cas_payload_round_trip` | NEW | `CasStore::put` → `CasStore::get` round-trip; CID stable across runs (post-CO1.4-extra cold-restart). |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:316:| `tests/sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` | un-ignore (currently `#[ignore = "CO1.7.5: requires real per-kind transition bodies"]` at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:1451`) | Submit 100 TypedTx through Sequencer; replay → byte-identical `state_root`. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:324:1. **Legacy `src/ledger.rs` retirement** — CO1.1.5 atom (per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`). CO1.7.5 leaves the legacy WAL ledger fully running; bus.rs / kernel.rs continue to call `self.ledger.append(...)` for non-typed events.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:327:4. **Submission queue back-pressure tuning** — Q2 fixed at deterministic exponential backoff (Q2 v1.1 resolution); CO1.7.5 inherits it. Per-task fairness deferred to v4.x.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:328:5. **STATE § 3 line 412 NodeId::from_state_root patch** — CO1.7.5 spec lands D1-D4. STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC v1.5 single-line clarification is a separate housekeeping commit (NOT re-audit; § 0.3 above).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:329:6. **Materializer state_root computation** — CO1.8 atom (L5). CO1.7.5 transition bodies build `q_next.state_root_t` via the same `q_next.economic_state_t.derive_state_root()` accessor that CO1.7-impl tests already exercise; CO1.8 will replace this with a real merkleized materializer.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:343:| **Q4 SignalBundle population** | **CLOSED**: shipped 4-variant `SignalKind` (Empty/Finalize/TaskExpired/TerminalSummary) suffices for all 7 transition bodies; full emit table in § 0.3.2 above. | Smoke read of `src/state/typed_tx.rs:830-854` confirmed CO1.1.4-pre1 minimization. STATE § 3 BoolSignal/StatSignal richness is CO1.9 scope; CO1.7.5 makes zero SignalKind additions. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:367:Coverage: 22 / 22 shipped variants accounted for (15 used + 7 indirectly: `NotYetImplemented` is the stub sentinel that disappears post-CO1.7.5; the others map 1-to-1). CO1.7.5 adds **zero** new variants.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:395:- **Spec rounds**: 1-2 expected (CO1.7.5 inherits frozen ABI; 5 of 6 v1 open Qs already closed by smoke; only Q1 remains as audit input — substantively smaller surface than CO1.7 v1's 11 must-fix items).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:401:- **Total atom budget**: ~1,000-1,560 LoC (down from initial 1,200-1,900 because Bus stays struct-shape-compatible after D3 simplification). **Estimated calendar time**: 4-7 days. Implementation may bundle as `CO1.7.5-impl A1+A2+A3+A4` like CO1.7-impl, OR ship as 4 sequential commits.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:408:1. **Two STATE § 3 supersessions adopted, not introduced** (§ 0.3): NodeId-from-commit-OID + 4-variant SignalKind. Both are CO1.7-K3-v1.2 and CO1.1.4-pre1 minimization decisions that already cleared dual audit; CO1.7.5 carries them forward unchanged. Auditors should validate the authority chain (CO1.7 v1.2 → CO1.7.5 vs STATE v1.4) — that's the substantive review surface, not the resolutions themselves.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:413:6. **FC-trace requirements** (per CLAUDE.md "Alignment Standard"): every NEW pub symbol introduced by CO1.7.5 must carry a doc-comment `/// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role>` backlink. The set is: 7 transition body fns (each → STATE § 3.x); `LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid_hex` (→ § 5 L4 sequencer post-commit head_t wiring); `Kernel.sequencer` field + `Kernel::with_sequencer` + `Bus::submit_typed_tx` (→ § 5.2.1 single-writer entry-point). `tests/fc_alignment_conformance.rs` witness-test additions are scoped under the implementation atom (not this spec).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:488:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:509:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:510:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:522:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:523:| C2 | spec did not acknowledge that shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes empty in-memory index → cold-replay impossible | § 0 + § 5 explicit dependency + mitigation: CasStore index persistence is deferred to **CO1.4-extra** (separate atom); v1 documents the gap | Codex Q-H + Gemini Q2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:528:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:531:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:547:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:548:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:555:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:560:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:740:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:771:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:863:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:875:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:900:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:932:- `CasMissing { at, cid }` (NEW; FullTransition only — fires if CO1.4-extra not yet landed)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:946:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:947:- **C2**: cold-replay availability requires `CasStore` index persistence; deferred to CO1.4-extra. Until then, full-mode replay errors with `CasMissing` if CAS state is not warm.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:964:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1202:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1203:| **P10** | TaskId-vs-TxId QState index mismatch (typed_tx uses TaskId; QState `task_markets_t` / `escrows_t` / `stakes_t` keyed by TxId) | This spec § 9 NEW D-4 documents the forward-migration plan: CO P2.1 (TaskMarket atom) owns the QState retrofit; v1.1 records the migration debt + cross-atom dependency note. Does NOT perform the retrofit (out of CO1.1.4-pre1 scope; would touch q_state.rs which is its own atom). | CX-3 (Codex Q-J) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1210:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1214:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1238:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1239:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1240:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1241:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1245:- `src/state/q_state.rs` (existing): keeps its existing types verbatim. CO1.1.4-pre1 only adds new types in `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1252:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1255:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1256:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1257:- CO1.4-extra has its own dual-audit gate.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1259:This ordering is a **necessary condition for CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS** per round-1 Gemini Q4; documented here so future audits cannot reinterpret silence as approval.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1360:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1416:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7855:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11103:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:34:-   Deferring the implementation of CAS index persistence to a new, dedicated atom (CO1.4-extra) is an architecturally sound decision. It unblocks CO1.7, correctly scopes the work, and documents the dependency, turning an implicit risk into a managed work item.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11104:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:50:**Q12 (NEW; scope expansion)**: The expansion is handled correctly. Spec §13 acknowledges the new CO1.4-extra atom and budgets it separately. This is proper dependency management, not scope creep within CO1.7. The approach is acceptable.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11106:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1:# Gemini CO1.7.5 transition_bodies_and_runtime_wiring Round-1 Audit Run
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11107:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:9:# Gemini CO1.7.5 Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11108:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15:CO1.7.5 demonstrates strong constitutional alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11109:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:19:-   **Art 0.2 Tape Canonical 公理**: The design upholds this axiom. The `Sequencer::apply_one` pseudocode in the inherited `CO1.7` spec (§ 3) and the shipped `sequencer.rs` (lines 310-315) show that the pure transition function is called *before* any commit action. If it returns an `Err`, the function returns early, and no `LedgerEntry` is committed. This ensures rejected transactions do not advance the ledger state, which holds for all 7 transition bodies as they are all routed through `dispatch_transition`. The `LedgerEntry.system_signature` attests to the sequencer-stamped semantics of the *accepted* transition, fulfilling the attestation requirement.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11110:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:21:-   **Art 0.4 Q_t version-controlled**: The design correctly implements the Art 0.4 binding. The spec's D2 deliverable (§ 1 D2) explicitly sets `q.head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)`. This is the direct implementation of Art 0.4's `HEAD_t = git commit SHA` principle. The spec correctly notes in § 0.3.1 that this supersedes the `STATE v1.4 § 3` `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` suggestion, citing the higher authority of `CO1.7 K3 v1.2 § 5`. This demonstrates correct interpretation of the constitutional hierarchy: the specific, audited implementation detail in a later spec (CO1.7) refines the general principle from an earlier one (STATE v1.4), and both align with the highest law (Constitution Art 0.4).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11111:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:29:The spec correctly identifies and carries forward two necessary supersessions from prior, audited specs (`CO1.7-K3-v1.2` and `CO1.1.4-pre1`). The legitimacy of a downstream, more specific, audited spec superseding an upstream, more general one is a valid and necessary principle for architectural evolution. The resolutions themselves are sound.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11112:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:35:**This constitutes a process-level challenge**: The spec must be amended to state that as part of the CO1.7.5 atom's closure, a formal change request or issue will be filed against the `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC` to align its pseudocode with the as-implemented reality of `head_t` mutation and `SignalKind` shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11113:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:41:CO1.7.5 correctly closes Wave 6 #1.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11114:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:71:-   **Safe Deferral**: This is a classic example of incremental delivery. It ships a functional L4 now, with a clear and safe path for adding L6 signal richness later. This reduces the complexity and risk of the CO1.7.5 atom.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11115:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:109:The CO1.7.5 spec is architecturally sound, constitutionally aligned, and demonstrates a mature understanding of the project's principles. The design correctly closes Wave 6 #1 while providing clear affordances for future waves.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11116:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:117:3.  **Clarify Supersession Authority and Responsibility (from Q2)**: The language in § 0.3 should be strengthened. Instead of merely documenting a carry-forward, it should assert the principle that a later, more specific, audited spec (like CO1.7) legitimately supersedes an earlier one (like STATE v1.4), while also acknowledging the institutional debt to file a housekeeping patch against the superseded document. This provides clarity on the project's governance of its own specifications.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11117:handover/audits/CODEX_SPEC_V14_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-27.md:15:Q5: CLOSED as a spec-freeze issue. § 2.5 freezes the canonical serialization rule: bincode v2, big-endian fixed ints, BTreeMap lexicographic keys, UTF-8 length-prefix strings, explicit Option and enum encodings. NEW-5: CLOSED; § 2.5 explicitly defers the full fixture corpus, differential fuzz seed, and complete QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError runner ABI to CO1.1.4-pre1 and CO1.7, so this is tracked downstream rather than left ambiguous. Q6: CLOSED. § 5.2.1 assigns logical_t through sequencer `next_logical_t()`, and § 5.2.6 makes the atomic assignment the canonical tie-break for concurrent submitters.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11124:handover/audits/CODEX_CO_P0_AUDIT_2026-04-26.md:75:| §6 transition protocol (`:422-515`) | No seed row | CO1.7.5 `src/transition/mod.rs` (`/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/architect-insights/CO_MEGA_PLAN_v3.1_2026-04-26.md:136`) | ORPHAN-IN-TRACE, not orphan-in-plan. Add Blueprint §6 row and test for reject path preserving `Q_t` while recording rejected tx (`:505-514`). |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11139:handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:42:| **K7** | **Conformance test gap** — spec promises 8; skeleton has 6 (none for signature, CAS recovery, sequencer serialization, full replay, canonical fixtures) | Q-H | Either lower spec promise (defer 2 tests to CO1.7.5+ along with body impls) OR add stubbed tests now. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11159:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:45:-   **A partial Art 0.2 implementation that should NOT be called "I-DETHASH witness" until full?**: **CHALLENGE**. Correct. Calling the current chain-integrity check a full "I-DETHASH witness" is an overstatement. It is a witness for *chain integrity*, not for *deterministic state transition*. The name should be qualified, for example, as an "I-DETHASH chain-integrity witness," until the full replay (spec § 4 `replay` function, including re-running pure transitions) is implemented in CO1.7.5+.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11163:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:40:| **P8** (C-3 + GM-2) | FinalizeRewardTx Q-derived discipline + dual-sign rationale | NEW § 4.1 Q-derived (task_id/solver/reward authoritative from Q at replay; royalty NOT on wire); NEW § 4.2 dual-sign (this struct's sig binds payload bytes; envelope sig binds sequencer-stamped bytes; both needed) | Q-derived discipline clearly stated? Replay rule (CO1.7-impl A4) committed? Dual-sign rationale convincing or still redundant? |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11164:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:41:| **P9** (GM-1 Gemini Q4) | Cold-replay Art 0.2 | NEW § 0.1 cross-atom ordering gate: v1.1 PASS contingent on CO1.4-extra shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 | Ordering gate explicit + binding? Constitutional commitment strong enough? |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11166:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:67:**Q-7. P8 + P9 closure (Q-derived + dual-sign + cold-replay gate)**: Spec § 4.1 commits replay (CO1.7-impl A4) to Q-derived discipline — is this binding enough? Spec § 0.1 cross-atom ordering gate phrased as "MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra" — strong constitutional commitment or hedged language?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11167:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:78:**Q-10. Implementation gating (overall)**: with v1.1 closure, is CO1.7-impl A2 (TypedTx + Sequencer + dispatch_transition) implementable end-to-end against this ABI surface? Specific blockers to call out.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11169:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:123:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11170:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:131:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11171:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:135:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11173:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:159:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11174:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:160:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11175:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:161:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11176:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:162:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11178:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:173:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11179:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:176:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11180:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:177:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11182:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:281:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11186:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:337:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11188:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:400:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11191:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:417:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11210:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2774:**Pre-implementation gate**: NOT cleared. v1.1 patch round required before CO1.7-impl A2 unblocks.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11211:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2800:| **GM-1** | **Art 0.2 cold-replay constitutional commitment** | Q4: payload data lives in CAS (referenced via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`); CO1.7 spec § 0 already deferred CAS index persistence to **CO1.4-extra**. Until that ships, cold-restart loses payload data → tape is non-canonical → Art 0.2 violation. v1.1 of CO1.1.4-pre1 must explicitly gate PASS on a concrete commitment to ship CO1.4-extra alongside or before CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). Strategic risk: shipping a constitutionally-non-compliant tape layer, even temporarily, is too high. |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11214:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2840:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11216:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2866:5. **Cold-replay → Art 0.2 commitment is a real cross-atom gate**: declaring "CO1.4-extra is a separate atom" is not enough; the deferred dependency creates a window where the tape is non-canonical. Sedimented: v1.1 must explicitly state the cross-atom ordering constraint (CO1.4-extra MUST ship before CO1.7-impl A4 replay_full_transition) and PASS is contingent on that ordering being honored.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11217:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2883:> - **§ 2.5 + § 7 fixture corpus defer-ack** (Q5/NEW-5): canonical serialization RULE frozen v1.4; full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzz seed lands in CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7 atoms (not v1.x spec scope).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11218:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3198:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11277:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7480:    20	| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11278:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7488:    28	**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11279:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7492:    32	**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11281:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7516:    56	- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11282:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7517:    57	- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11283:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7518:    58	- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11284:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7519:    59	- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11286:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7530:    70	**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11287:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7533:    73	- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11288:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7534:    74	- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11289:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7604:   178	If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11292:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7629:   234	`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11293:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:7722:   324	**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11532:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:245:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11533:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:253:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11534:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:257:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11536:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:281:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11537:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:282:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11538:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:283:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11539:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:284:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11541:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:295:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11542:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:298:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11543:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:299:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11545:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:403:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11549:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:459:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11551:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:545:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11554:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND5_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:562:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11627:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11628:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:21:# Codex Audit — CO1.7-impl BUNDLE Round-1 (Pre-CO1.7.5 implementation gate)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11629:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:25:**Mandate**: round-1 dual external audit on the **CO1.7-impl bundle** (A1 + A2 + A3 + A4) + **CO1.4-extra** atom, all mid-implementation commits. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" + spec § 12: this PASS/PASS gates entry to CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring to bus.rs/kernel.rs).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11630:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:34:| **A2** Sequencer | `609d8d5` | ~290 (in 430 incl. tests) | 5 | K1 dual counter (next_submit_id / next_logical_t); tokio mpsc bounded queue; apply_one 9 stages per spec § 3; K3 head_t deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring; ApplyError wrapper (impl deviation from spec § 3 line 307 — TransitionError stays closed-taxonomy + additive-only per CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11631:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:35:| **A3** dispatch_transition | `609d8d5` | ~30 | 1 | Exhaustive 7-variant enum match; every variant returns NotYetImplemented stub (CO1.7.5 fills) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11632:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:36:| **A4** replay_full_transition | `272fcf4` | ~140 + LedgerCasView trait | 4 (1 #[ignore]) | 9-stage full-mode replay; new ReplayError::Transition variant; LedgerCasView trait (DIV-4 closure); tests prove stages 1-6 PASS, stage 7 stubs; #[ignore]'d test for full state_root reconstruction (CO1.7.5) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11633:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:37:| **CO1.4-extra** | `b6b7574` | ~110 + tests | 5 | Sidecar JSONL CAS index persistence; closes Art 0.2 cold-replay gate per CO1.1.4-pre1 § 0.1 cross-atom ordering |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11636:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:49:- **PASS/PASS** unblocks **CO1.7.5** (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring to bus.rs/kernel.rs); CO1.7.5 is the final L4 atom
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11637:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:57:**Q-B. A2 Sequencer K1 invariant + K3 deferred head_t**: K1 — submit advances submit_id; commit advances logical_t. Verify rejected-submission path does NOT advance logical_t (test `apply_one_stub_does_not_consume_logical_t` covers stub mode; what about future real-transition rejection path)? K3 — head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+. Is leaving `q.head_t` un-mutated (still at QState::default empty string) during the entire CO1.7-impl runtime ACCEPTABLE for Art 0.4 (Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩) compliance? Or is this a gap that should at least set head_t to a placeholder pointing at the latest commit OID?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11638:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:63:**Q-E. A3 dispatch_transition exhaustive match contract**: every variant returns NotYetImplemented. Is this the right pattern for "transition body not yet implemented", or should the stubs use unimplemented!() macro for clearer crash semantics? (Tests verify the Err return path; CO1.7.5 fills bodies preserving the match arms.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11639:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:78:- Test gaps: anything from STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 4 (27 invariants) that CO1.7-impl bundle should provide a witness for but doesn't?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11640:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:81:**Q-L. Implementation gating**: with bundle at 237/0 PASS, is CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition bodies + STEP_B bus.rs/kernel.rs wiring) implementable end-to-end against this bundle? Specific blockers to call out.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11641:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:85:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11644:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:112:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11648:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:133:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11649:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:134:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11655:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:171:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11656:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:172:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11658:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:179:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11659:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:184:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11668:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:487:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11669:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:499:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11670:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:524:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
