CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11700:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:834:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11701:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:838:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11703:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:862:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11704:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:863:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11705:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:864:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11706:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:865:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11708:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:876:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11709:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:879:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.

Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11856:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11749:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11857:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11804:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:39:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11858:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11805:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:47:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11859:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11806:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:51:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11860:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11807:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:76:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11861:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11808:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:78:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11863:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11810:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:89:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11864:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11811:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:93:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11865:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11812:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:197:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11867:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11816:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:3:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11869:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11818:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:25:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11871:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11820:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:37:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11872:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11821:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:38:| C2 | spec did not acknowledge that shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes empty in-memory index → cold-replay impossible | § 0 + § 5 explicit dependency + mitigation: CasStore index persistence is deferred to **CO1.4-extra** (separate atom); v1 documents the gap | Codex Q-H + Gemini Q2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11874:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11823:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:43:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11876:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11825:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:62:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11877:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11826:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:63:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11878:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11827:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:70:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11879:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11828:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:75:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11884:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11833:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:286:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11891:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11840:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:378:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11896:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11845:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:390:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11908:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11857:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:447:- `CasMissing { at, cid }` (NEW; FullTransition only — fires if CO1.4-extra not yet landed)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11912:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11861:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:461:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11913:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:11862:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:462:- **C2**: cold-replay availability requires `CasStore` index persistence; deferred to CO1.4-extra. Until then, full-mode replay errors with `CasMissing` if CAS state is not warm.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11949:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12098:a03cc52 CO1.7-impl A1: Git2LedgerWriter + canonical bincode codec
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11953:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12192:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11954:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12206:K3 deferral is acceptable for this gate because the spec explicitly defers `head_t`; do not set a placeholder commit OID. CO1.7.5 must wire it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11955:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12246:Blocking: `replay_full_transition` does not accept a genesis `QState`; it accepts only two roots and fabricates `QState::genesis()` ([transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:348), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:368)). That drops all non-root genesis state: budget, registries, balances, task markets. CO1.7.5 cannot reconstruct real state from this API.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11956:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12254:CO1.7.5 is close, but not gate-ready. The transition bodies can be written, but full replay and sequencer commit semantics need the above patches first.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11957:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12270:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11958:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12284:K3 deferral is acceptable for this gate because the spec explicitly defers `head_t`; do not set a placeholder commit OID. CO1.7.5 must wire it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11959:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12324:Blocking: `replay_full_transition` does not accept a genesis `QState`; it accepts only two roots and fabricates `QState::genesis()` ([transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:348), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:368)). That drops all non-root genesis state: budget, registries, balances, task markets. CO1.7.5 cannot reconstruct real state from this API.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11960:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:12332:CO1.7.5 is close, but not gate-ready. The transition bodies can be written, but full replay and sequencer commit semantics need the above patches first.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11961:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:1:# Gemini CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit Run
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11962:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8:# Gemini CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-1 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11963:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15:- **Art 0.4 Q_t = ⟨q_t, HEAD_t, tape_t⟩ version-controlled**: The implementation explicitly does NOT mutate `head_t`, leaving it at `QState::default` (`sequencer.rs:356-361`). The CO1.7 spec v1.2 § 3 explicitly defers this mutation to CO1.7.5+ wiring (R2-K3 closure). While this is a temporary deviation from the full constitutional vision of Art 0.4, the implementation correctly adheres to the audited spec. The deviation is documented, scoped, and has a planned resolution. It is an acceptable interim state, not an implementation defect. **PASS**.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11964:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:26:The primary "missing" piece is the body of the transition functions themselves (`dispatch_transition` stubs), which is explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5. This bundle delivers the L4 *framework* or *scaffolding* as envisioned in WP § 5. The stubs act as a clear contract for the next atom. There are no visible omissions in the L4 machinery itself that would surface as a structural gap in Wave 6. **PASS**.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11965:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:37:The deferral of `head_t` mutation presents a latent risk of observable inconsistency. For the duration of CO1.7-impl runtime, any component querying `q.head_t` will receive a stale, default empty string.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11967:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:41:The risk becomes acute if CO1.7.5 is delayed or if parallel atoms are developed that *do* assume a valid `head_t`. The state is acceptable *only* as a transient, short-lived condition with a hard dependency on CO1.7.5 for resolution. The implementation is correct per the spec, but the strategic risk is significant enough to warrant a **CHALLENGE**. The system is in a temporarily non-constitutional state, and while planned, this must be tracked with the highest priority.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11968:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:46:## Q6 CO1.7.5 unblock contract
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11969:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:47:The next atom, CO1.7.5, has a clear contract based on this bundle:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11971:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:52:The current bundle does not present any visible omissions that would block CO1.7.5. The `LedgerWriter` trait's return of `Hash` and the separate `Git2LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid()` method is a workable, if slightly indirect, interface for CO1.7.5 to consume. The path is clear. **PASS**.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11972:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:67:1.  **`head_t` Deferral Debt**: The most critical risk. The temporary constitutional deviation regarding `head_t` is now concrete in the code. This creates a high-priority technical debt that, if not paid promptly in CO1.7.5, could lead to subtle state inconsistencies as other system components are built.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11973:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:76:1.  **Must-Fix: `head_t` Constitutional Alignment.** The CHALLENGE can only be resolved when CO1.7.5 is delivered, implementing the `head_t` mutation to reflect the true git commit SHA per Art 0.4. This is the highest priority architectural item.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11974:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:119:**Q8. PASS gate**: does v1.2 close all R2 findings cleanly enough for CO1.7-impl A2 unblock?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11976:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:180:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11977:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:188:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11978:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:192:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11980:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:216:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11981:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:217:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11982:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:218:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11983:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:219:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11985:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:230:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11986:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:233:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11987:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:234:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11989:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:338:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11993:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:394:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11995:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:480:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11998:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:497:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.

This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:12595:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:8351:-   **A partial Art 0.2 implementation that should NOT be called "I-DETHASH witness" until full?**: **CHALLENGE**. Correct. Calling the current chain-integrity check a full "I-DETHASH witness" is an overstatement. It is a witness for *chain integrity*, not for *deterministic state transition*. The name should be qualified, for example, as an "I-DETHASH chain-integrity witness," until the full replay (spec § 4 `replay` function, including re-running pure transitions) is implemented in CO1.7.5+.

Both auditors agree the spec-level deferral is acceptable for this gate; closure happens in CO1.7.5 atom. **Documented as a CO1.7.5 hard prerequisite** in the next atom's spec. |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13501:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6248:     1	CO1.7-impl bundle v1.1.1 + round-2 audit (CHALLENGE → 2 missing tests added)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13503:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6299:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-3 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13505:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:6322:# Codex CO1.7-impl Bundle Round-3 Audit
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13508:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:58:**Q8. PASS gate**: does v1.2 close all R2 findings cleanly enough for CO1.7-impl A2 unblock?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13510:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:119:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13511:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:127:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13512:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:131:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13514:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:155:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13515:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:156:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13516:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:157:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13517:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:158:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13519:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:169:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13520:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:172:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13521:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:173:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13523:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:277:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13532:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:436:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13645:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:144:CO1.7.5 inherits two prior minimization decisions that diverge from STATE § 3 v1.4 pseudocode. Both were ratified by downstream specs reaching dual-audit PASS/PASS; CO1.7.5 carries them forward unchanged. Re-audit gating for the STATE spec itself is **not** decided here — CO1.7.5 spec only documents the carry-forward; whether STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC needs a v1.5 housekeeping commit is a separate decision for the STATE spec curator.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13646:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:149:| STATE § 3 line 412 (and parallel lines in § 3.1 line 467, § 3.2 line 561) | `q_next.head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(new_state_root)` inside the pure transition body | STATE v1.4 round-4 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13647:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:150:| CO1.7 K3 v1.2 § 5 | "`NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version"; head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ Sequencer post-commit | CO1.7 v1.2 round-3 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13648:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:152:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies **never** mutate `q_next.head_t`. Pure function returns `q_next` with `head_t == q.head_t`. `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` happens exclusively in `Sequencer::apply_one` post-commit (§ 1 D2 below). All 7 transition bodies inherit this rule.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13649:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:157:| STATE § 3 lines 403-409 (and parallels in § 3.1 / § 3.2) | `SignalBundle { boolean: vec![Signal::Boolean(BoolSignal::AcceptedAt(...))], statistical: vec![Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::PriceUpdate(...)), Signal::Statistical(StatSignal::ReputationDelta(...))] }` (two-axis vec with Bool/Stat sub-variants) | STATE v1.4 round-4 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13650:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:158:| CO1.1.4-pre1 § 7.2 + shipped `src/state/typed_tx.rs:830-854` | `SignalBundle { kind: SignalKind }` with 4 variants only: `Empty / Finalize { claim_id, reward } / TaskExpired { task_id, bounty_refunded } / TerminalSummary { run_id, outcome }` | CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13651:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:160:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies emit `SignalBundle` matching the **shipped** 4-variant SignalKind only. Per the CO1.1.4-pre1 doc-comment "Full L6 signal-stream design is CO1.9", the BoolSignal/StatSignal richness is deferred. Shipped emit table:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13652:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:172:CO1.9 will extend SignalKind with the deferred reputation / price-update / acceptance-event variants; CO1.7.5 makes **zero** SignalKind additions (preserves CO1.1.4-pre1 ABI lock).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13653:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:174:### 0.4 Pre-implementation gate (from CO1.7 § 12)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13654:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:175:> "CO1.7 must reach PASS/PASS before implementing CO1.7.5 (transition function bodies) + CO1.4-extra (CAS persistence)."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13655:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:177:CO1.7-impl reached PASS/PASS-equivalent at commit `2461fe6` (per LATEST.md 2026-04-28 session-2 summary). CO1.4-extra shipped at commit `b6b7574`. **Pre-implementation gate is therefore satisfied**; CO1.7.5 spec is unblocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13656:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:208:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13657:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:218:// NEW (CO1.7.5 D2): head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13658:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:224:**Atomicity** (extends v1.1 C-2 closure): under the acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and two field assignments (infallible). There is no failure point between commit success and `head_t` advancement; `q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` therefore advance atomically with `next_logical_t`. The single-writer per (runtime_repo, run_id) invariant from CO1.7 § 5.2.1 is preserved.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13659:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:226:**Required trait extension**: the existing `LedgerWriter` trait does **NOT** expose `head_commit_oid()` (only `Git2LedgerWriter` has it). CO1.7.5 adds:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13660:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:234:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5): canonical 40-char lowercase hex commit OID of the most
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13661:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:251:**Why `if let Some(...)` instead of `unwrap()`**: tolerates `InMemoryLedgerWriter` deterministically. CO1.7.5+ never panics on `None`; it simply leaves `head_t` stale, which is acceptable for unit tests (they assert on `ledger_root_t` only, per the CO1.7-impl test suite).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13662:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:255:`src/bus.rs:9` and `src/kernel.rs:8` both use legacy `crate::ledger::{Ledger, Node, NodeId, EventType, TapeError}` (retirement is CO1.1.5's job, not CO1.7.5's). `Bus::new(kernel: Kernel, config: BusConfig)` (bus.rs:87) shows Bus owns Kernel; the architecture below exploits this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13663:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:263:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5 D3): None when kernel runs in legacy-only mode.

Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13697:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:510:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13698:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:522:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13699:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:527:| K3 | L4/L5 head_t ownership inconsistent (spec line 194 vs 276 disagreed) | CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` + commit-chain `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` only; L5 (CO1.8) owns `state_root_t` mutation; sequencer drops `head_t = NodeId::from_state_root(...)` line | Codex Q-E |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13700:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:528:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13701:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:531:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13702:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:541:### In scope (CO1.7 atom)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13703:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:547:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13704:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:548:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13705:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:554:- system_keypair signing primitives — done @ CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 only adds a typed extension.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13706:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:555:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13707:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:560:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13708:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:563:- `src/ledger.rs` (legacy, top-level) is retired in **CO1.1.5 (kernel.rs split)**; CO1.7 lives at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs` (NEW). No STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required (new module, not restricted file); restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard" are `src/{kernel,bus,wallet}.rs` (corrected from v1's incorrect `wal.rs` per K6 tail).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13709:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:692:CO1.7 extends shipped `system_keypair::CanonicalMessage` with one new variant. **R2-C3 design choice**: variant carries the **opaque 32-byte canonical_digest** of `LedgerEntrySigningPayload`, NOT the full payload struct. This avoids a circular `system_keypair ↔ transition_ledger` module dependency (the payload struct needs `Cid` from CAS module + `SystemEpoch` from system_keypair; carrying the precomputed digest sidesteps the cycle entirely). The signature still binds the full payload because `canonical_digest()` is deterministic in `transition_ledger`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13710:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:734:├── system_keypair.rs            (existing CO1.7.0a-f; CO1.7 adds 1 enum variant + 1 typed sign fn — additive)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13711:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:739:├── q_state.rs                   (existing; CO1.7 fills `ledger_root_t` placeholder; does NOT touch `state_root_t` per K3)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13712:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:740:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13713:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:743:**Crate boundary**: `transition_ledger` in `bottom_white::ledger` (tool layer); `sequencer` in `state::` (touches Q_t mutation). Sequencer DEPENDS ON ledger; ledger does NOT depend on sequencer (DAG: state → bottom_white::ledger → CO1.4 CAS → CO1.7.0a-f keypair).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13714:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:767:    /// **C3**: signing key handle (CO1.7.0a-f).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13715:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:771:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13716:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:863:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13717:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:875:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13718:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:900:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13719:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:915:/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13720:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:946:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13721:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:964:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13722:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:968:**Implementation (CO1.7.5+)**: `Git2LedgerWriter` (built on existing CO1.4 CAS); skeleton `InMemoryLedgerWriter` for v1 testing.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13732:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1040:CO1.7 lives in NEW files (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs`, future `src/state/sequencer.rs`). It does NOT modify `src/bus.rs` / `src/kernel.rs` / `src/wallet.rs` (the STEP_B-restricted files per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard"; v1 incorrectly listed `wal.rs`). Therefore: **no STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony required** for the CO1.7 atom itself.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13733:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1047:Future runtime wiring (CO1.7.5+) into `bus.rs`/`kernel.rs` WILL need STEP_B — that's a separate atom. The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13745:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1162:# XREF: CO1.1.4-pre1 spec (frozen, PASS/PASS) — supplies ABI lock authority
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13746:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1168:**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13747:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1202:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13748:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1210:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13749:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1214:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13750:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1237:- **Slash transition** — already deferred to CO P2.5 ChallengeCourt per CO1.7 spec K5.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13751:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1238:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13752:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1239:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13753:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1240:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13754:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1241:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13755:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1250:**Constitutional concern**: CO1.7 LedgerEntry stores typed-tx payloads in L3 CAS via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`. The current shipped `CasStore::open()` initializes an empty in-memory index (CO1.4 store.rs:67); after process restart the CAS bytes are unrecoverable until the index is repopulated. This means **cold-replay of L4 cannot reconstruct typed payloads** — a direct Art. 0.2 (tape canonicality) violation if uncorrected.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13756:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1252:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13757:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1255:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13758:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1256:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13759:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1335:**Spec gap**: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 3.4 uses `FinalizeTx::from(claim_id, reward)` constructor pattern but provides no explicit struct. CO1.7 spec § 1 lists `TxKind::FinalizeReward = 4` but defers the struct to "frozen in STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1" — which the STATE spec doesn't actually contain.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13760:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1360:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13761:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1368:- L4 `LedgerEntry.system_signature` signs the **sequencer-stamped envelope** (`LedgerEntrySigningPayload.canonical_digest()` via `b"turingosv4.ledger_entry_signing.v1"` — CO1.7 spec § 1.2). Audit-relevant for: "this `(logical_t, parent_ledger_root, tx_payload_cid)` was committed by the sequencer".
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13762:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1378:`TxStatus` includes a `Pending` variant (per STATE § 1.2) but in this v4 codebase `TxStatus` is **set BY the runner**, never serialized into the canonical transaction wire format. Therefore: `TxStatus` is **NOT a field of any TypedTx variant**; it is a runtime book-keeping enum exposed on the public API surface but not part of the canonical encoding. (CO1.7 spec § 1.2 puts `status: TxStatus` on WorkTx field 12; this atom **diverges**: status is tracked in `q_t.q_t.agents[id].last_accepted_tx` + ClaimsIndex, NOT on the wire. **Audit input**: confirm or push back.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13763:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1410:The `TxKind` enum already exists in `transition_ledger.rs` with `#[repr(u8)]` and explicit discriminants. `TypedTx::tx_kind()` is the projection used by CO1.7 sequencer apply_one stage 5 (`tx_kind: TxKind::from_typed(&tx)` → renamed `TypedTx::tx_kind(&tx)` for ergonomics).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13764:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1416:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13765:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1500:| 4+ | … | … | … | iterate to PASS/PASS |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13766:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1502:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13767:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1504:**Audit cost estimate**: ~$15-25 (smaller surface than CO1.7 spec @ $25-42; mostly type definitions + 2 plausibly-derived schemas).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13768:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1513:- **Total atom budget**: ~1.5-2.5 days from spec draft to PASS/PASS.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13769:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1519:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13770:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1521:3. **Versioning**: `extensions: BTreeMap<String, Vec<u8>>` is on `LedgerEntry` (CO1.7); per-tx forward compat is via additive variants on `TypedTx` (e.g. `TypedTx::MetaTx(...)` lands in v4.1). No per-struct `version` field.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13771:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1531:# XREF: STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC v1.4 (frozen, round-4 PASS/PASS) — pseudocode authority for D1
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13772:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1541:> - **§ 2.5 + § 7 fixture corpus defer-ack** (Q5/NEW-5): canonical serialization RULE frozen v1.4; full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzz seed lands in CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7 atoms (not v1.x spec scope).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13773:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:1856:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13835:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7855:CO1.7.5 spec § 7 smoke S5 surfaced this. Sending a spec to dual external audit while the referenced restricted-file list contradicts repo state is a guaranteed CHALLENGE — a wasted round. Per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, smoke discrepancies block audit launch. Fixing the docs at smoke time is the cheapest path; deferring would leak the inconsistency into the audit prompt.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13866:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8145:   101	In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13867:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8155:   111	// NEW (CO1.7.5 D2): head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13868:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8161:   117	**Atomicity** (extends v1.1 C-2 closure): under the acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and two field assignments (infallible). There is no failure point between commit success and `head_t` advancement; `q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` therefore advance atomically with `next_logical_t`. The single-writer per (runtime_repo, run_id) invariant from CO1.7 § 5.2.1 is preserved.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13869:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8163:   119	**Required trait extension**: the existing `LedgerWriter` trait does **NOT** expose `head_commit_oid()` (only `Git2LedgerWriter` has it). CO1.7.5 adds:

CO1.7.5+ never panics on `None`; it simply leaves `head_t` stale, which is acceptable for unit tests (they assert on `ledger_root_t` only, per the CO1.7-impl test suite).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13949:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:11065:     5	**Status (v1)**: v1 DRAFT, post-CO1.7 PASS/PASS gate (2026-04-28).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13950:handover/audits/CO1_1_4_PRE1_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:18:**Pre-implementation gate**: NOT cleared. v1.1 patch round required before CO1.7-impl A2 unblocks.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13951:handover/audits/CO1_1_4_PRE1_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:44:| **GM-1** | **Art 0.2 cold-replay constitutional commitment** | Q4: payload data lives in CAS (referenced via `tx_payload_cid: Cid`); CO1.7 spec § 0 already deferred CAS index persistence to **CO1.4-extra**. Until that ships, cold-restart loses payload data → tape is non-canonical → Art 0.2 violation. v1.1 of CO1.1.4-pre1 must explicitly gate PASS on a concrete commitment to ship CO1.4-extra alongside or before CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). Strategic risk: shipping a constitutionally-non-compliant tape layer, even temporarily, is too high. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13952:handover/audits/CO1_1_4_PRE1_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:71:| **P9**: spec patch — § 0 add explicit "Art 0.2 cold-replay gate": v1.1 PASS contingent on CO1.4-extra commitment (explicit cross-atom dependency; CO1.7-impl A4 must NOT ship before CO1.4-extra) | GM-1 | spec § 0 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13954:handover/audits/CO1_1_4_PRE1_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:84:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13956:handover/audits/CO1_1_4_PRE1_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:110:5. **Cold-replay → Art 0.2 commitment is a real cross-atom gate**: declaring "CO1.4-extra is a separate atom" is not enough; the deferred dependency creates a window where the tape is non-canonical. Sedimented: v1.1 must explicitly state the cross-atom ordering constraint (CO1.4-extra MUST ship before CO1.7-impl A4 replay_full_transition) and PASS is contingent on that ordering being honored.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13962:handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:50:-   **CRITICAL FLAW:** The spec for CO1.7 (§0) and this atom's context correctly identify that if CAS bytes are lost, the system enters a state of partial verifiability. The L4 `LedgerEntry` chain can be verified (signatures over CIDs are intact), but the state transitions cannot be replayed, as the payloads are gone. This **violates Art 0.2 (Tape Canonical 公理)**, which demands that "all signals must be reconstructible from the tape." A tape of pointers to missing data is not sufficient for reconstruction. While the fix (CAS index persistence) belongs to CO1.4-extra, this atom cannot PASS without a concrete, committed, and immediate plan to resolve this constitutional violation. The current state introduces a dangerous "trust mode" ambiguity.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13994:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:43:| R2-K3 | head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists. v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only. `LedgerWriter::commit` keeps `Hash` return. Spec § 0/§ 3/§ 5 updated; "CO1.7 owns head_t" claim removed. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:13996:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:60:- § 5 storage backend says head_t deferred to CO1.7.5+
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14000:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:101:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14004:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:122:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14005:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:123:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14006:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:135:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14008:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:141:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14009:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:144:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14011:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:160:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14012:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:161:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14014:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:168:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14015:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:173:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14020:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:353:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14023:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:384:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14024:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:476:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14025:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:488:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14026:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:513:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14028:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:559:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14093:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2429:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14349:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10597:-   **A partial Art 0.2 implementation that should NOT be called "I-DETHASH witness" until full?**: **CHALLENGE**. Correct. Calling the current chain-integrity check a full "I-DETHASH witness" is an overstatement. It is a witness for *chain integrity*, not for *deterministic state transition*. The name should be qualified, for example, as an "I-DETHASH chain-integrity witness," until the full replay (spec § 4 `replay` function, including re-running pure transitions) is implemented in CO1.7.5+.
