Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14556:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15058:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:24:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14557:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15059:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:25:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14565:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15067:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:63:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14567:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15069:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:70:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14594:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15096:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:378:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14597:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15099:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:390:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14598:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15100:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:461:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14601:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:15103:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:519:| `tests/system_signature_verifies_via_canonical_message` | CO1.7.5+ | LedgerEntry.system_signature verifies through `verify_system_signature(&CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(...), epoch, pinned_pubkeys)` |

Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.

Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |

Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |

Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.

Per (b) decision (atom split, not absorption): CO1.1.4-pre1 ships the ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, before A2-A4 unblock.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14904:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:41:- **PASS** unblocks CO1.7-impl A2 (TypedTx + transition stubs → Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition); and is a transitive dependency of CO P2.x economy atoms (TaskId / AgentSignature / etc. are reused).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14905:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:63:**Q-D. Bincode v2 settings & cross-platform stability**: CO1.7-impl A1 (commit `a03cc52`) froze `bincode_canonical_config()` = `standard().with_big_endian().with_fixed_int_encoding()`. The CO1.1.4-pre1 tests reuse this codec via `canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode`. Concerns:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14906:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:72:**Q-F. SignalBundle minimal v1 — does it actually work for CO1.7.5?**: spec § 0 says "minimal SignalBundle: a single enum-like discriminator + payload sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile". Implementation: `pub struct SignalBundle { pub kind: SignalKind }` with 4 variants (Empty/Finalize/TaskExpired/TerminalSummary).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14907:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:73:- Does CO1.7.5 step_transition / verify_transition / challenge_transition / reuse_transition need additional variants this v1 didn't anticipate?
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14908:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:78:- Is the `NotYetImplemented` sentinel a code smell that audit should flag? (It's the explicit interface CO1.7.5 will fill in; documented in spec § 0.)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14910:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:124:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14911:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:128:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14913:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:152:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14914:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:153:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14915:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:154:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14916:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:155:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14920:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:302:`canonical_encode` / `canonical_decode` (already shipped in `transition_ledger.rs` per CO1.7-impl A1) are reused as the wire codec:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14922:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:348:**Pre-implementation gate** (for CO1.7-impl A2-A4): CO1.1.4-pre1 must reach `PASS/PASS` before A2 starts.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14925:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:365:1. **Field-level meaning beyond identifier types**: e.g. what `read_set` MUST contain for replay attribution to work — that's a CO1.7.5 + CO P2.4.0 concern.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14936:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:832:/// compile and for CO1.7.5 transition bodies to construct each variant.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14942:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2676:> - **§ 2.5 + § 7 fixture corpus defer-ack** (Q5/NEW-5): canonical serialization RULE frozen v1.4; full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzz seed lands in CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7 atoms (not v1.x spec scope).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14943:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:2991:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14946:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3757:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14950:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3778:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14951:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3779:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14952:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3791:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14954:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3797:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14955:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3800:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14957:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3816:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14958:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3817:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14960:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3824:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14961:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:3829:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14966:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4009:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14969:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4040:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14970:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4132:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14971:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4144:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14972:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4169:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14973:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4184:/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14974:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4215:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:14975:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:4233:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15016:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5504:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:5:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15017:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5513:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:35:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15018:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5514:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:36:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15021:handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_1_4_PRE1_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:5564:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15088:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:3:**Status**: v1.2 — round-2 returned PASS (Gemini, high) + CHALLENGE (Codex, high; 3 narrow patch blockers). Conservative merged CHALLENGE. v1.2 closes the 3 v1.1→v1.2 patches: (a) C3 actually wired in code (`CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` + `transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry`); (b) K3 head_t mutation explicitly deferred to CO1.7.5+ (no longer claimed in v1.x); (c) `ObjectType::Transition` replaced with shipped `ObjectType::ProposalPayload`. Plus typo fix and 1 new test. Awaiting round-3.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15092:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:24:| **R2-C3** | Spec claimed "C3 CLOSED" but `system_keypair.rs` had no LedgerEntry path; skeleton itself said "deferred to CO1.7.5+" | Wave 4-B additive extension shipped: `CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning([u8;32])` (opaque digest variant; avoids transition_ledger ↔ system_keypair circular dep) + `canonical_digest` match arm + new `pub(crate) mod transition_ledger_emitter` with `sign_ledger_entry(keypair, digest)`. Skeleton test 9 (`signature_round_trip_and_transplant_defense`) now exercises the real roundtrip + K2 + D1 defenses. | Codex round-2 must-fix #1 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15093:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:25:| **R2-K3** | Spec § 3 / § 5 said "CO1.7 owns head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)" but `LedgerWriter::commit` returns `Hash` not commit SHA; v1.1 InMemoryLedgerWriter has no commit_sha to return at all → contradiction | head_t mutation explicitly **deferred to CO1.7.5+** (when Git2LedgerWriter exists and can return both Hash + commit SHA). v1.x ledger owns `ledger_root_t` only; `head_t` continues to be set elsewhere (currently QState placeholder; CO1.7.5 wiring concern). Spec § 0 / § 3 / § 5 updated. | Codex round-2 must-fix #2 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15094:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:37:| C1 | replay was single-mode; called "I-DETHASH witness" but skeleton only did chain check | Two-mode `ReplayMode::ChainOnly` (skeleton-stage) vs `ReplayMode::FullTransition` (CO1.7.5+; I-DETHASH witness only in this mode) | Codex Q-D + Gemini Q3 |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15096:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:43:| K4 | spec `LedgerWriter::commit(&self) → NodeId` + `iter_from` did not match skeleton `commit(&mut self) → Hash` | Spec aligned to skeleton: `&mut self` + `Hash` return; `iter_from` deferred to CO1.7.5+ when needed for cold-replay | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15097:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:46:| K7 | spec promised 8 conformance tests; skeleton has 6 | Explicit list of 8 tests with skeleton-stage vs CO1.7.5-stage marker; unimplemented stubs now stage-marked | Codex Q-H |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15099:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:62:- **replay (two-mode)**: `ChainOnly` (chain integrity; skeleton-stage; v1) vs `FullTransition` (rerun pure transitions + verify state_root + verify signatures; CO1.7.5+; THE I-DETHASH witness).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15100:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:63:- **Storage backend**: git2-rs commit chain (built on CO1.4 CAS); each LedgerEntry = one git commit on `refs/transitions/main`. **R2-K3**: head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+ — v1.x ledger does NOT mutate `Q_t.head_t` directly. Once `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` exists and returns commit_sha alongside Hash, CO1.7.5 wiring will set `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` outside the L4 sequencer apply path.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15102:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:70:- L5 materializer (state_root computation) — deferred to **CO1.8**. **K3 boundary (revised v1.2)**: CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only; CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`; **head_t mutation is deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring** (when `Git2LedgerWriter` exists). Sequencer does NOT mutate `state_root_t` or `head_t` directly; it accepts `q_next.state_root_t` as returned by the transition function and persists `ledger_root_t`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15103:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:75:- **CAS index persistence (cold-replay enabler)** — `CasStore::open()` shipped at Wave 3 initializes empty in-memory index ([store.rs:67](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/cas/store.rs)); cold-replay therefore cannot recover payloads via `CasStore::get` after restart. **CO1.4-extra** atom (NEW, scheduled post-CO1.7) adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest). v1 ledger documents the dependency; full-mode replay is implementable once CO1.4-extra lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15108:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:255:└── sequencer.rs                 (NEW; deferred to CO1.7.5; pre-audit type stub may land in v1.1 if useful)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15111:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:286:    /// Storage backend (in CO1.7.5+; skeleton uses InMemoryLedgerWriter).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15112:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:378:        // `ledger_root_t` only. head_t mutation is **deferred to CO1.7.5+ wiring**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15113:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:390:**Why no head_t mutation in apply_one (K3, revised v1.2)**: v1.x CO1.7 owns `ledger_root_t` only. CO1.8 owns `state_root_t`. **head_t mutation deferred to CO1.7.5+** when `Git2LedgerWriter` provides a commit_sha return alongside Hash; the InMemoryLedgerWriter used by the v1 skeleton has no commit_sha to expose, so the trait keeps a single `Hash` return and head_t wiring is a separate downstream concern. Sequencer never calls `NodeId::from_state_root(...)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15114:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:415:    /// CO1.7.5+ stage: full re-execution.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15115:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:430:/// CO1.7.5+ stage entry point (v1.1 spec only; impl deferred).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15116:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:461:- **K3 (v1.2)**: `head_t = NodeId(commit_sha)` is the canonical convention WHEN head_t is wired (CO1.7.5+). v1.x sequencer does NOT mutate head_t — `Git2LedgerWriter` is needed to surface commit_sha. `NodeId::from_state_root(...)` is NOT used by L4 in any version.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15117:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:479:    // K4: iter_from() deferred — used only by FullTransition replay; CO1.7.5+ adds it.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15118:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:483:**Implementation (CO1.7.5+)**: `Git2LedgerWriter` (built on existing CO1.4 CAS); skeleton `InMemoryLedgerWriter` for v1 testing.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15127:handover/specs/CO1_7_TRANSITION_LEDGER_v1_2026-04-28.md:523:**v1 stage (skeleton)**: 8 tests (6 already in skeleton + 2 NEW K2/Q9). **CO1.7.5+ stage**: 4 more.

The retirement of `src/ledger.rs` (legacy top-level) is in CO1.1.5 per `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 5.3`.

Diagnostic-collection mode is a v4.x extension; first-error simplicity matches CO1.7.5 implementation budget. |

All addressed. |

Sedimented per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard" (Generator ≠ Evaluator) + memory `feedback_dual_audit`.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15144:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:9:> - **§ 2.5 + § 7 fixture corpus defer-ack** (Q5/NEW-5): canonical serialization RULE frozen v1.4; full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzz seed lands in CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7 atoms (not v1.x spec scope).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15145:handover/specs/STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md:324:**Out of scope for v1.x spec** (deferred per Codex Q5/NEW-5 round-3 PARTIAL acknowledgment): full golden fixture corpus + differential fuzzing seed + complete runner ABI for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError. v1.4 freezes the SERIALIZATION RULE (bincode v2 big-endian + BTreeMap lex); fixtures + ABI land in **CO1.1.4-pre1** (canonical fixture corpus) + **CO1.7** (full ABI surface). This is an **explicit deferral** — not unresolved spec ambiguity. STEP_B branch A and branch B both implement the SAME bincode rule; per-tx digest matching is mechanical from v1.4. Full corpus generation is a downstream code task, not spec scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15147:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:39:| **P9** | Cold-replay → Art 0.2 violation if CAS index not persisted | This spec § 0 NEW "Cross-Atom Ordering Gate": v1.1 PASS is contingent on CO1.4-extra (CAS index persistence) shipping BEFORE CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition). CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) and A3 (dispatch_transition stubs) may proceed; A4 BLOCKED on CO1.4-extra. | GM-1 (Gemini Q4) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15148:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:47:**Why this atom exists**: spec § 2.5 of `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC_v1_2026-04-27.md` explicitly deferred "full ABI surface for QState/SignalBundle/TransitionError" to CO1.7. CO1.7 spec § 0 places the per-kind tx schemas in `STATE_TRANSITION_SPEC § 1` ("frozen on paper, not yet in code"). When CO1.7-impl A1 (Git2LedgerWriter, commit `a03cc52`) shipped, downstream A2 (TypedTx + dispatch_transition) discovered ~30 supporting schema types are required but **none of them exist in code** — only `MicroCoin` is defined. This atom defines that ABI surface in isolation under its own dual-audit gate, per the project's per-atom audit principle (CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard").
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15149:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:51:**Single sentence**: define every supporting type + the 7 typed-tx variant payload structs + the `TypedTx` enum, with `Serialize/Deserialize` derives over the spec § 2.5 canonical encoding (bincode v2 BE + fixed_int), so that CO1.7-impl A2-A4 (Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition) can be implemented against a stable type surface.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15151:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:75:- **Per-kind transition function bodies** (`step_transition`, `verify_transition`, `challenge_transition`, `reuse_transition`, `finalize_reward_transition`, `task_expire_transition`, `emit_terminal_summary_transition`) — these consume the ABI defined here; they belong to **CO1.7.5** (the body atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15152:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:76:- **Sequencer + dispatch_transition + replay_full_transition** — these consume the ABI; they belong to CO1.7-impl **A2-A4** (post this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15153:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:77:- **`SignalBundle` typed shape** — STATE spec uses `SignalBundle::empty()` / `::finalize(...)` / `::task_expired(...)` / `::terminal_summary(...)` constructors. v1 of this atom emits a minimal typed `SignalBundle` (single enum-like discriminator + payload) sufficient for CO1.7-impl to compile; full event-stream design lands in CO1.9 L6 signal indices.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15154:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:78:- **TransitionError full taxonomy** — v1 emits a minimal enum covering the variants invoked in spec § 3 pseudocode (`ClaimNotFound`, `ChallengeWindowStillOpen`, `AlreadySlashed`, `TaskNotFound`, `InvalidSystemSignature`, `StaleParent`, `TaskNotExpired`, `TaskHasOpenClaim`, `TerminalSummaryNotApplicable`, `NotYetImplemented`); per-stage enum proliferation is a CO1.7.5 concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15156:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:89:**Mitigation**: CAS index persistence is its own atom — **CO1.4-extra** — already named in CO1.7 spec § 0. CO1.4-extra adds index persistence (likely a sidecar JSONL or git-tag manifest) so cold-replay can recover payloads via `CasStore::get`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15157:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:92:- CO1.7-impl A2 (Sequencer apply path) + A3 (dispatch_transition skeleton) may proceed against CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.1 PASS independently.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15158:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:93:- **CO1.7-impl A4 (replay_full_transition) MUST NOT ship before CO1.4-extra**. Until then, FullTransition replay errors with `CasMissing` after process restart (already documented in CO1.7 spec § 4 / `ReplayError::CasMissing`).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15160:handover/specs/CO1_1_4_PRE1_TYPED_TX_ABI_v1_2026-04-28.md:197:If wire-stored values diverge from Q-derived values at replay, **replay rejects with `TransitionError::ClaimNotFound` or a stricter mismatch error** (CO1.7-impl A4 enforces this; CO1.7.5 transition body owns the comparison rule).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15187:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:45:**Resolution carried forward**: CO1.7.5 transition bodies **never** mutate `q_next.head_t`. Pure function returns `q_next` with `head_t == q.head_t`. `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` happens exclusively in `Sequencer::apply_one` post-commit (§ 1 D2 below). All 7 transition bodies inherit this rule.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15193:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:68:> "CO1.7 must reach PASS/PASS before implementing CO1.7.5 (transition function bodies) + CO1.4-extra (CAS persistence)."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15194:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:70:CO1.7-impl reached PASS/PASS-equivalent at commit `2461fe6` (per LATEST.md 2026-04-28 session-2 summary). CO1.4-extra shipped at commit `b6b7574`. **Pre-implementation gate is therefore satisfied**; CO1.7.5 spec is unblocked.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15195:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:101:In `src/state/sequencer.rs::apply_one`, stage 9 currently mutates `q.ledger_root_t` from `entry.resulting_ledger_root` (lines 362-373) but leaves `q.head_t` unchanged (the K3 v1.2 deferral). CO1.7.5 closes this. Two `NodeId` types coexist in the codebase — the legacy `pub type NodeId = String` at `src/ledger.rs:13` and the new `pub struct NodeId(pub String)` at `src/state/q_state.rs:49`. `q.head_t` is typed as the **new** `state::q_state::NodeId` (tuple struct); CO1.7.5 D2 constructs the new variant exclusively. Legacy `crate::ledger::NodeId` is untouched by D2.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15196:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:111:// NEW (CO1.7.5 D2): head_t = state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15197:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:117:**Atomicity** (extends v1.1 C-2 closure): under the acquired `q_w` + `writer_w` write locks, after `writer_w.commit(&entry)?` returns `Ok`, the remaining operations are an `AtomicU64::store` (infallible), a plain `*q_w = q_next` move (infallible), and two field assignments (infallible). There is no failure point between commit success and `head_t` advancement; `q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` therefore advance atomically with `next_logical_t`. The single-writer per (runtime_repo, run_id) invariant from CO1.7 § 5.2.1 is preserved.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15198:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:119:**Required trait extension**: the existing `LedgerWriter` trait does **NOT** expose `head_commit_oid()` (only `Git2LedgerWriter` has it). CO1.7.5 adds:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15199:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:127:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5): canonical 40-char lowercase hex commit OID of the most
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15200:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:144:**Why `if let Some(...)` instead of `unwrap()`**: tolerates `InMemoryLedgerWriter` deterministically. CO1.7.5+ never panics on `None`; it simply leaves `head_t` stale, which is acceptable for unit tests (they assert on `ledger_root_t` only, per the CO1.7-impl test suite).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15201:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:148:`src/bus.rs:9` and `src/kernel.rs:8` both use legacy `crate::ledger::{Ledger, Node, NodeId, EventType, TapeError}` (retirement is CO1.1.5's job, not CO1.7.5's). `Bus::new(kernel: Kernel, config: BusConfig)` (bus.rs:87) shows Bus owns Kernel; the architecture below exploits this.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_5_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:15202:handover/specs/CO1_7_5_TRANSITION_BODIES_AND_RUNTIME_WIRING_v1_2026-04-29.md:156:    /// NEW (CO1.7.5 D3): None when kernel runs in legacy-only mode.

Per CO1.1.4-pre1, EconomicState mutations within transition bodies happen via `q.economic_state_t.{balances_t, stakes_t, escrows_t, claims_t}.method_call()` types under `src/economy/` (not the wallet tool). Smoke S5 verifies 0 hits. CLAUDE.md + STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md path drift (`src/wallet.rs` → `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`) was sedimented and fixed in `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` during smoke phase; the STEP_B-restricted set now correctly reads `src/kernel.rs` + `src/bus.rs` + `src/sdk/tools/wallet.rs`.

     1	# CO1.7-impl Bundle Dual External Audit — Final Merged Verdict ✅ PASS/PASS-equivalent
     2	
     3	**Date**: 2026-04-28
     4	**Atom**: CO1.7-impl bundle (A1+A2+A3+A4) + CO1.4-extra
     5	**Final state**: v1.1.1 (commit `1bc8887` impl + `[next]` doc-banner refresh)
     6	**Conservative rule** (memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS.
     7	
     8	---
     9	
    10	## § 1 Final verdicts
    11	
    12	| Auditor | Final round | Verdict | Conviction |
    13	|---|---|---|---|
    14	| **Codex** | r3 (v1.1.1) | **PASS** | High |
    15	| **Gemini** | r1 (v1.0) | CHALLENGE → carry-forward to CO1.7.5 | High |
    16	| **Conservative merged** | — | **PASS-equivalent** ✅ (with G-1 head_t deferred per spec design) | High |
    17	
    18	**Pre-CO1.7.5 implementation gate**: CLEARED. CO1.7.5 (per-kind transition function bodies + STEP_B wiring) is now unblocked.
    19	
    20	---
    21	
    22	## § 2 Round-by-round summary
    23	
    24	| Round | Codex | Gemini | Conservative | Patch Round Output |
    25	|---|---|---|---|---|
    26	| 1 | CHALLENGE/high (3 must-fix + secondary) | CHALLENGE/high (1 carry-forward + 2 risks) | CHALLENGE | v1.1: P1-P4 (4 patches); commit `1a921e5` |
    27	| 2 (Codex-only) | CHALLENGE/high (2 missing tests claimed-not-shipped) | (carry-forward Gemini r1) | CHALLENGE | v1.1.1: 2 missing tests added; commit `1bc8887` |
    28	| 3 (Codex-only) | **PASS/high** | (carry-forward Gemini r1) | **PASS** ✅ | doc-banner refresh; this commit |
    29	
    30	---
    31	
    32	## § 3 Closure verification (cumulative)
    33	
    34	| Codex r1 must-fix | Closure |
    35	|---|---|
    36	| C-1: replay_full_transition signature drops genesis QState | P1: signature → `(genesis: &QState, ...) -> Result<QState, ReplayError>` |
    37	| C-2: K1 violated under infra failure | P2: `next_logical_t.store()` deferred until AFTER `writer.commit` succeeds |
    38	| C-3: replay never asserts tx_kind match | P3: NEW `ReplayError::TxKindMismatch`; stage 6.5 assertion + test (added v1.1.1) |
    39	| C-3-secondary: decode error reported as CasMissing | P4: NEW `ReplayError::PayloadDecode`; stage 6 distinguishes; test (added v1.1.1) |
    40	
    41	| Gemini r1 must-fix / risks | Status |
    42	|---|---|
    43	| G-1: head_t Art 0.4 alignment | DEFERRED to CO1.7.5 per CO1.7 spec K3 v1.2 (already PASS/PASS at spec level); both auditors agreed deferral acceptable |
    44	| G-2: CO1.4-extra O(N) startup latency | TRACKED as long-term tech debt (instrument + monitor in production; embedded-DB upgrade open for post-Wave 6 scale) |
    45	| G-3: apply_one ↔ replay divergence hazard | PARTIALLY MITIGATED via C-3 (tx_kind match closes one specific divergence vector); general engineering discipline going forward |
    46	
    47	---
    48	
    49	## § 4 Cumulative cost
    50	
    51	| Round | Codex tokens | Gemini tokens | Estimated $ |
    52	|---|---|---|---|
    53	| Bundle r1 | 189,349 | 137,536 | ~$8-15 |
    54	| Bundle r2 (Codex-only) | 67,977 | — | ~$3-5 |
    55	| Bundle r3 (Codex-only) | 124,574 | — | ~$3-5 |
    56	| **Bundle total** | **381,900** | **137,536** | **~$14-25** |
    57	
    58	**CO1.7-impl bundle total cost**: ~$14-25. Cumulative project audit spend: ~$175-273 / $890 mid-budget (~20-31%).
    59	
    60	The bundle audit cost (~$14-25 across 3 rounds) is **substantially cheaper than per-atom audit** would have been (4 atoms × ~$8-15 each = ~$32-60). Bundling validated.
    61	
    62	---
    63	
    64	## § 5 What CO1.7.5 inherits
    65	
    66	**Code surface frozen**:
    67	- `Sequencer` (state/sequencer.rs) with full apply_one machinery; only the per-kind transition bodies are stubbed
    68	- `dispatch_transition` exhaustive 7-variant match
    69	- `replay_full_transition` 9-stage I-DETHASH witness (incl. tx_kind match + decode-vs-CAS-miss separation)
    70	- `Git2LedgerWriter` production storage backend
    71	- `LedgerCasView` trait + CasStore impl
    72	- `CasStore` with sidecar JSONL persistence (CO1.4-extra)
    73	
    74	**CO1.7.5 must deliver**:
    75	1. Real per-kind transition function bodies for the 7 TypedTx variants in `dispatch_transition` (currently `Err(NotYetImplemented)`)
    76	2. **Close G-1 head_t Art 0.4 alignment** — wire `q.head_t = NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` after `Git2LedgerWriter::commit` returns; `Git2LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid()` already exposed for this
    77	3. STEP_B parallel-branch ceremony for any modifications to `bus.rs` / `kernel.rs` (per CLAUDE.md "Code Standard")
    78	4. Once bodies land: remove `#[ignore]` from `sequencer_serial_replay_byte_identity` test; verify final state_root reconstruction end-to-end
    79	
    80	---
    81	
    82	## § 6 Sedimented lessons (cumulative across rounds)
    83	
    84	1. **Bundle audits work for tight-clustered atoms**: 4 atoms in 1 audit gave concrete actionable findings without focus dilution. Net 50%+ saving vs per-atom audit. Sedimented: when atoms share an architectural design and ship together, bundle the audit.
    85	
    86	2. **K1 invariant requires "all-or-nothing" on counter advance**: monotonic counters that gate load-bearing invariants MUST advance only on full critical-section success. Spec § 3 stage-4 fetch_add was wrong; correct pattern is `load → use → store after final fallible step`.
    87	
    88	3. **Envelope-vs-payload integrity check is a real attack surface**: signing the envelope's `tx_kind` doesn't verify the dereferenced CAS payload's actual variant. Round-1 CHALLENGE caught this; the 5-LOC fix in stage 6.5 closes a class of envelope-payload swap attacks.
    89	
    90	4. **Replay APIs are forward-locked**: a deceptively-small reduction in API shape (state_root + ledger_root vs full QState) loses downstream consumer capability irretrievably once shipped. Err on more-state-than-less for first cuts.
    91	
    92	5. **Carry-forward CHALLENGEs are a real verdict pattern**: Gemini's #1 head_t was unfixable in this atom by spec design (K3 v1.2 deferred). The conservative-merge result is still CHALLENGE, but the action is "track + close in next atom" not "fix + re-audit". Documented as carry-forward in merged verdict.
    93	
    94	6. **Claim-vs-code parity drift recurs across atoms**: round-2 CHALLENGE caught 2 tests claimed in v1.1 commit message but never actually landed (silent Edit failure). Same lesson as CO1.1.4-pre1 doc-hygiene rounds. Sedimented: post-implementation, before audit dispatch, run a programmatic check that "claimed identifiers exist in source".
    95	
    96	7. **Codex-only narrow rounds are efficient closure mechanism**: when Gemini has already accepted the design and only patch-mechanical defects remain, dispatch Codex-only saves ~$3-5/round. CO1.1.4-pre1 + CO1.7-impl bundle both used this pattern successfully.
    97	
    98	— ArchitectAI synthesis, 2026-04-28; CO1.7-impl bundle PASS/PASS-equivalent gate cleared 14:12 UTC.

Real value: 2 spec bugs (Q9 cycle, K2 transplant), 5 spec-vs-code divergences (DIV-1..5), 1 module-dep design improvement (opaque digest variant), all caught before any wasted CO1.7-impl work.

    86	
    87	1. **Spec-only audit is insufficient for module-integrative atoms**: round-1 paper review missed DIV-1..5 + Q9 cycle + transplant attack vector — all surfaced via type-skeleton smoke. Sedimented: per memory `feedback_smoke_before_batch`, EVERY spec-driven atom should ship a type-skeleton smoke before round-1 audit.
    88	
    89	2. **"Claim-vs-code" parity is a real audit failure mode**: round-2 Codex caught spec saying "C3 CLOSED" while system_keypair.rs had no LedgerEntry path. Sedimented: spec patch logs must reference actual git diffs, not aspirational language.

    36	All 18 round-1 + round-2 must-fix items are CLOSED.
    37	
    38	| Codex r5 question | Result |
    39	|---|---|
    40	| Q1 `system_keypair.rs:229` doc | PASS — `TerminalSummarySigningPayload::canonical_digest()` |
    41	| Q2 `typed_tx.rs:218` doc | PASS — `WorkSigningPayload::canonical_digest()` via `WorkTx::to_signing_payload()` |
    42	| Q3 `typed_tx.rs:336` doc | PASS — `TerminalSummaryTx::to_signing_payload().canonical_digest()` + domain prefix |
    43	| Q4 Other stale residue | PASS — exact residue greps for 6 candidate patterns: NO MATCHES |
    44	| Q5 Test status | PASS — 224/0 lib PASS, 0 ignored |
    45	
    46	---
    47	
    48	## § 4 Cumulative Cost
    49	
    50	| Round | Codex tokens | Gemini tokens | Estimated $ |
    51	|---|---|---|---|
    52	| 1 | 199,200 | 113,295 | ~$8-15 |
    53	| 2 | 165,930 | 114,610 | ~$7-13 |
    54	| 3 | 91,030 | 109,082 | ~$4-9 |
    55	| 4 (Codex-only) | 121,526 | — | ~$3-6 |
    56	| 5 (Codex-only) | 159,562 | — | ~$4-7 |
    57	| **CO1.1.4-pre1 total** | **737,248** | **336,987** | **~$26-50** |
    58	
    59	Cumulative project audit spend: ~$161-252 / $890 mid-budget (~18-28%).
    60	
    61	CO1.1.4-pre1 audit cost is in the **mid-range** (CO1.7 spec was $25-42 across 3 rounds; CO1.1.4-pre1 was $26-50 across 5 rounds — the extra two rounds were Codex-only doc-hygiene closure passes, not full design rounds). Real value: 1 critical security gap closed (agent-sig domain separation), 4 derived schemas locked (FinalizeRewardTx + dual-sign rationale; TerminalSummaryTx full migration; ClaimId newtype; bincode codec wording fix), 1 constitutional gate established (Art 0.2 cross-atom ordering for CO1.4-extra), 5 sedimented lessons captured in round-1/round-2 verdicts.
    62	
    63	---
    64	
    65	## § 5 Implementation path (now unblocked)
    66	
    67	Per CO1.7-impl R3 § 5 + spec § 13:
    68	
    69	1. **CO1.7-impl A2** (Sequencer + transition stubs): now unblocked. ~150 LoC. Builds on TypedTx defined in this atom + Sequencer per CO1.7 spec § 3.
    70	2. **CO1.7-impl A3** (dispatch_transition exhaustive enum match): blocked-by A2. ~50 LoC stubs returning `TransitionError::NotYetImplemented`.
    71	3. **CO1.4-extra** (CAS index persistence): MUST ship before CO1.7-impl A4 per this atom's § 0.1 cross-atom ordering gate. Independent atom.
    72	4. **CO1.7-impl A4** (replay_full_transition + 4 CO1.7.5-stage tests): blocked-by A2 + A3 + CO1.4-extra. ~200-300 LoC.
    73	5. **CO1.7.5** (per-kind transition function bodies): final L4 atom; fills the `NotYetImplemented` stubs from A3 with real transition logic.
    74	
    75	Total downstream from CO1.1.4-pre1 PASS: **5 atoms** estimated 6-12 days for full Wave 6 #1 closure.
    76	
    77	---
    78	
    79	## § 6 Sedimented lessons (cumulative across all 5 rounds)
    80	
    81	1. **Single-source-of-truth for tx schemas matters** (round-1): TerminalSummaryTx living in `system_keypair.rs` (signer module) leaked into a "frozen" location. Sedimented: per-typed-tx struct lives in `state::typed_tx`; signers consume opaque digests.
    82	
    83	2. **Spec wording must specify codec-library byte layout** (round-1): STATE § 2.5 v1.4 was wrong (`#[repr(u8)]` does not control serde wire format). Sedimented: when freezing a wire format, spec must include actual codec library version + verified-by-test byte layout.
    84	
    85	3. **"Record-only" golden fixtures are not golden** (round-1): self-comparison ("two encodes match") is round-trip stability, not ABI freezing. Sedimented: every ABI-freeze atom MUST lock SHA-256 hex in v1.
    86	
    87	4. **Domain separation is non-negotiable when one signature primitive serves multiple roles** (round-1): type-level distinction at the API surface is necessary but not sufficient; canonical_digest pre-image MUST encode the role via stable byte-prefix.
    88	
    89	5. **Cold-replay → Art 0.2 cross-atom ordering must be explicit in spec** (round-1): declaring "X is a separate atom" is not enough; create explicit cross-atom ordering gate (CO1.4-extra MUST ship before CO1.7-impl A4).
    90	
    91	6. **Single-call-site type-update is insufficient** (round-2): adding ClaimId newtype but missing SignalBundle::Finalize. Sedimented: when changing a tx-payload field type, grep for ALL consumers (incl. SignalBundle / runtime APIs / fixtures).
    92	
    93	7. **Symmetric-API completion** (round-2): adding `TerminalSummarySigning` variant but not corresponding `FinalizeRewardSigning` / `TaskExpireSigning` for the other system-emitted txs. Sedimented: when introducing a typed signing primitive for one variant, confirm symmetric coverage for ALL siblings.
    94	
    95	8. **Domain-prefix tests must use IDENTICAL bodies** (round-2): non-collision test using DIFFERENT bodies passes trivially even without domain prefix. Sedimented: load-bearing domain-separation tests MUST construct identical bytes, hash with each domain, assert distinct results.
    96	
    97	9. **Spec drift after structural migration** (round-2): claim "row removed" must be verified with grep. Same pattern as CO1.7 R2-C3.
    98	
    99	10. **Codex implementation discipline + Gemini design quality is a stable axis decomposition** (rounds 2+3): Gemini PASSed at v1.1 + v1.2; Codex CHALLENGEd both with concrete patch lists. CHALLENGEs were closure-mechanical, not foundational disagreements. PASS/PASS only when both axes are clean. Project pattern.
   100

   238	
   239	    /// Submit a typed transition. Returns immediately with a receipt carrying
   240	    /// `submit_id` (NOT `logical_t`). Per Q2 (back-pressure resolution): on
   241	    /// queue saturation returns `Err(SubmitError::QueueFull)` and the agent is
   242	    /// expected to retry with deterministic exponential backoff.
   243	    pub async fn submit(&self, tx: TypedTx) -> Result<SubmissionReceipt, SubmitError> {
   244	        let submit_id = self.next_submit_id.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
   245	        match self.queue_tx.try_send(tx) {
   246	            Ok(()) => Ok(SubmissionReceipt { submit_id }),
   247	            Err(tokio::sync::mpsc::error::TrySendError::Full(_)) => Err(SubmitError::QueueFull),
   248	            Err(tokio::sync::mpsc::error::TrySendError::Closed(_)) => Err(SubmitError::QueueClosed),
   249	        }
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    /// Driver loop. Drains the queue and runs `apply_one` on each tx. Errors
   253	    /// from individual `apply_one` calls are logged and skipped (per-tx
   254	    /// rejection does NOT halt the sequencer). Returns when the queue is
   255	    /// closed and drained.
   256	    pub async fn run(
   257	        &self,
   258	        mut queue_rx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<TypedTx>,
   259	    ) -> Result<(), SequencerError> {
   260	        while let Some(tx) = queue_rx.recv().await {
   261	            // Stub state: dispatch returns NotYetImplemented; apply_one
   262	            // bubbles up. We log and continue per spec § 3 v1.2 ordering rule
   263	            // (rejection does not consume a logical_t — see K1).
   264	            if let Err(e) = self.apply_one(tx) {
   265	                log::debug!("sequencer apply_one rejected: {e}");
   266	            }
   267	        }
   268	        Ok(())
   269	    }
   270

   275	    /// **only on commit success** — the original spec § 3 stage-4

    76	
    77	/// L1 PredicateRegistry — a deterministic ordered store of predicate metadata.
    78	///
    79	/// Uses BTreeMap (not HashMap) per spec § 2 I-BTREE invariant.
    80	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    81	pub struct PredicateRegistry {
    82	    predicates: BTreeMap<String, PredicateMetadata>,
    83	}
    84	
    85	#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
    86	pub enum RegisterError {
    87	    /// A predicate with this id already registered (use `replace` for explicit replacement).
    88	    DuplicateId(String),
    89	    /// Empty / malformed predicate_id.
    90	    InvalidId(String),
    91	}
    92	
    93	impl PredicateRegistry {
    94	    pub fn new() -> Self {
    95	        Self {
    96	            predicates: BTreeMap::new(),
    97	        }
    98	    }
    99	
   100	    /// Register a NEW predicate. Returns DuplicateId if id exists.
   101	    pub fn register(&mut self, meta: PredicateMetadata) -> Result<(), RegisterError> {
   102	        if meta.predicate_id.is_empty() {
   103	            return Err(RegisterError::InvalidId(meta.predicate_id));
   104	        }
   105	        if self.predicates.contains_key(&meta.predicate_id) {
   106	            return Err(RegisterError::DuplicateId(meta.predicate_id));
   107	        }
   108	        self.predicates.insert(meta.predicate_id.clone(), meta);
   109	        Ok(())
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    /// Lookup by predicate_id.
   113	    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&PredicateMetadata> {
   114	        self.predicates.get(id)
   115	    }
   116	
   117	    /// Total count of registered predicates.
   118	    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
   119	        self.predicates.len()
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    /// Whether the registry is empty.
   123	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
   124	        self.predicates.is_empty()
   125	    }
   126	
   127	    /// Compute Merkle-style root over all registered predicates' canonical hashes.
   128	    /// Returns sha256 of empty bytes if registry is empty (matches spec § 5.L1 EMPTY_TREE_ROOT).
   129	    pub fn merkle_root(&self) -> [u8; 32] {
   130	        let mut h = Sha256::new();
   131	        // BTreeMap iterates in lexicographic key order — deterministic.
   132	        for (_id, meta) in &self.predicates {
   133	            h.update(meta.canonical_hash());
   134	        }
   135	        h.finalize().into()
   136	    }
   137	
   138	    /// Agent-visible projection of the registry (Goodhart shield per Inv 10).
   139	    /// Returns a NEW registry containing only Public predicates + commit-reveal that have reveal-time passed.
   140	    pub fn agent_visible_view(&self, now: u64) -> Self {
   141	        Self {
   142	            predicates: self
   143	                .predicates
   144	                .iter()
   145	                .filter(|(_, m)| m.visibility.content_visible_to_agent(now))
   146	                .map(|(k, v)| (k.clone(), v.clone()))
   147	                .collect(),
   148	        }
   149	    }
   150	}

   225	pub enum CanonicalMessage {
   226	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-Sig: predicate-runner rejection summary.

   229	    /// summary signing-payload digest. Opaque `[u8; 32]` produced by

   240	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-Sig+FC3-Sig (CO1.1.4-pre1 v1.2 closure R2-2): task

   127	    pub fn register(&mut self, meta: ToolMetadata) -> Result<(), RegisterError> {
   128	        if meta.tool_id.is_empty() {
   129	            return Err(RegisterError::InvalidId(meta.tool_id));
   130	        }
   131	        if self.tools.contains_key(&meta.tool_id) {
   132	            return Err(RegisterError::DuplicateId(meta.tool_id));
   133	        }
   134	        // I-NOSIDE prerequisite: NonIdempotent tools cannot enter v4 registry.

   136	        if meta.determinism_class == DeterminismClass::NonIdempotent {
   137	            return Err(RegisterError::NonIdempotentNotAllowed(meta.tool_id));
   138	        }
   139	        self.tools.insert(meta.tool_id.clone(), meta);
   140	        Ok(())

# Codex CO1.7.5 Round-1 Audit

## Q-A Two-supersession framework
Authority chain is acceptable for CO1.7.5: CO1.7 v1.2 is the narrower L4 ledger spec and explicitly defers `head_t` mutation to CO1.7.5 (`CO1_7`:25, 70, 390, 461). CO1.1.4-pre1 is the ABI lock for `SignalBundle`. I do not think this requires Phase Z′, but v1.1 should state that STATE v1.5 housekeeping is required or at least tracked, because STATE remains a frozen cited authority.

Code-consistency: yes. Shipped `SignalKind` is exactly 4 variants at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:830-854`.

Forward-compat: mostly neutral. CO1.7.5 makes no enum additions (`spec:65`), so CO1.9 can append variants later. It should explicitly say historical L6 indexing may be recomputed/versioned, since signals are not stored in `LedgerEntry`.

## Q-B D2 head_t close
`crate::state::q_state::NodeId(commit_oid_hex)` type-checks: `NodeId` is a public tuple struct at `src/state/q_state.rs:47-49`, and `QState.head_t` uses it at `src/state/q_state.rs:310-311`. Legacy `src/ledger.rs:13` is only a type alias.

Atomicity is mostly real for the Git2 path, but the spec overclaims. After `writer_w.commit(&entry)?`, `AtomicU64::store`, `*q_w = q_next`, and field assignments are not fallible (`src/state/sequencer.rs:362-372`). However, `head_commit_oid_hex()` is still a trait dispatch after commit, and the default-`None` path intentionally leaves `head_t` stale (`spec:112-144`). So “`q.head_t` and `q.ledger_root_t` advance atomically” is only true for a Git2 writer that overrides and returns `Some`.

Default `None` is better than `unimplemented!()`: a panic after commit success would violate the post-commit no-failure goal. But v1.1 should require an explicit Git2 override plus a Git2-backed test.

## Q-C D3 combined STEP_B
Combined ceremony is permissible, but the spec overstates the protocol. STEP_B Phase 0 asks auditors for the “minimum sufficient version” (`STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:9-17`); it does not bind ceremony scoping by itself. A combined A/B unit is still reasonable because `bus.rs` and `kernel.rs` are coupled.

Architecture has compile and boundary defects:
- Actual bus type is `TuringBus`, not `Bus` (`src/bus.rs:53`, `src/bus.rs:86-87` vs spec `:171`).
- `Kernel` derives `Debug, Serialize, Deserialize` (`src/kernel.rs:18`). Adding `Option<Arc<Sequencer>>` as specified (`spec:154-157`) will not compile without `serde(skip)` and Debug handling; `Sequencer` has no derives at `src/state/sequencer.rs:190-207`.
- Kernel currently documents itself as “pure topology” (`src/kernel.rs:15-17`). Putting the L4 sequencer inside it needs stronger justification or a runtime-owner alternative.

Failure policy is specified: either file diverges → “re-do with stricter spec” (`spec:188`).

## Q-D D1 transition body purity
CHALLENGE. The purity contract (`spec:92-95`) does not match the inherited pseudocode or shipped APIs.

Concrete blockers:
- `challenge_transition` reads CAS inside the transition: `cas::get(&tx.counterexample_cid)?` (`STATE:506-508`). That violates “no I/O” and D1’s four-argument signature.
- `emit_terminal_summary_transition` takes `runtime: &Runtime`, reads run state, and signs inside the transition (`STATE:828-847`). That violates the `(&QState, &TxVariant, &PredicateRegistry, &ToolRegistry)` contract.
- System signature verification needs `PinnedSystemPubkeys` (`src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:501-506`), but D1 does not pass it.
- Agent signature verification is also underspecified; `typed_tx.rs` explicitly says agent pubkey lookup is a forward dependency (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:375`).
- Predicate execution APIs in STATE (`run_acceptance`, `run_verification`, `run_counterexample_check`) do not exist in shipped `PredicateRegistry`, which only has register/get/root/view methods (`src/top_white/predicates/registry.rs:93-150`).
- `spec:222` claims `q_next.economic_state_t.derive_state_root()` exists and is exercised. It does not exist in `src/`.

HashMap/f64: the cited `task_markets_t.get(...).map(...)` is not iteration and QState uses `BTreeMap`; legacy `prediction_market.rs` is f64-heavy (`src/prediction_market.rs:21-27`, `87-133`) but should stay outside transition call trees.

## Q-E Q5 mapping table completeness
CHALLENGE. The 22-variant enum exists (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:717-788`), but the mapping table overclaims.

Misses / ambiguities:
- CAS lookup failure in `challenge_transition` (`STATE:506-508`) has no mapped `TransitionError`.
- `SettlementEngine::issue_provisional(...) ?` in Work (`STATE:376-382`) is not mapped.
- Runtime / system-signature validation for `FinalizeRewardTx` and `TerminalSummaryTx` is not specified despite `InvalidSystemSignature`.
- Stale-parent checks for system txs are incomplete in the table; `TaskExpire` has one (`STATE:727-730`).

Dead / deferred variants: `NotYetImplemented` should disappear. Several others are only triggerable after missing QState/economy schemas exist.

The “RejectedAttemptSummary side channel” is not real enough to justify minimal payloads. A type exists at `src/bottom_white/ledger/system_keypair.rs:151-158`, but it is not the STATE shape (`STATE:192-214`) and Sequencer rejection currently only logs and skips (`src/state/sequencer.rs:252-266`). No rejected-summary stamping path is wired.

## Q-F Q4 SignalKind closure
`Empty` is acceptable as an ABI-minimization for CO1.7.5, not as a full L6 design. Reputation and price changes must still be in `QState` where STATE mutates them (`STATE:529-531`, `402-409`). If CO1.9 needs event richness, it should append SignalKind variants or build a separate versioned L6 indexer. CO1.7.5 should not amend CO1.1.4-pre1 now.

## Q-G Spec ↔ smoke/patch consistency
Smoke S1-S8 pass at HEAD `334111a`: `cargo check --workspace && cargo test --workspace --lib` passed with `239 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored` for `turingosv4`.

Defects:
- Footer says smoke ran at `2f5093a` (`spec:335`), not current HEAD `334111a`.
- D4 cites ignore at `transition_ledger.rs:1451` (`spec:209`), but the actual `#[ignore]` is line `1455`; line `1451` is the doc comment.
- S8 says “18 warnings” (`spec:346`); workspace check also emits one `gix_capability_spike` warning.
- P5-P12 are materially present (`spec:359-366`, `CLAUDE.md:14`, `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md:3`). P3 is stale wording: it references “§ 6 ack #8” but §6 now has 6 items.

## Q-H New defects
CO1.7-impl “PASS/PASS-equivalent” holds: final merged verdict says PASS-equivalent and clears the pre-CO1.7.5 gate (`handover/audits/CO1_7_IMPL_BUNDLE_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_FINAL_2026-04-28.md:1-18`). `2461fe6` exists and is on `main`.

`Git2LedgerWriter::head_commit_oid()` is exposed exactly at `src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:672-675`.

LoC estimate is not defensible as written. Current QState economy is mostly skeletal: e.g. `ClaimsIndex` is `BTreeMap<TxId, ClaimEntry>` with only `amount` and `claimant` (`src/state/q_state.rs:199-215`); `TaskMarketEntry` has no deadline/creator/config fields needed by STATE (`src/state/q_state.rs:230-238`); `ChallengeCase` lacks duration/outcome/is_open (`src/state/q_state.rs:273-285`). Either CO1.7.5 must own these schema/method additions, or it must declare CO P2.x blockers.

Also, the `LedgerWriter` trait snippet in the spec omits existing `read_at` (`src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:189-193` vs spec `:123-137`).

## Q-I Implementation gating
Not implementable end-to-end from v1 without scope clarification. The blockers are not in CO1.4-extra / CO1.7-impl / CO1.1.4-pre1 inheritance:
- QState/economic schemas and mutation methods needed by STATE §3.x.
- Deterministic state-root derivation until CO1.8.
- Pure-boundary handling for CAS counterexamples, terminal summaries, and signature verification.
- D3 runtime wiring compile details.

Once those are addressed in v1.1, the atom is implementable; no VETO-level design flaw found.

## **VERDICT**: CHALLENGE

## Top 3 must-fix
1. Specify the missing QState/economic/state-root implementation substrate: add required touch files and minimal schemas/methods, or declare hard dependencies on CO P2.x/CO1.8.
2. Repair the D1 purity boundary: no CAS/runtime/signing/key lookup inside pure transition bodies unless passed as deterministic inputs or pre-resolved into tx/QState.
3. Fix D3 wiring spec to compile against actual `TuringBus`/`Kernel` derives, or move Sequencer ownership to a runtime layer with a precise STEP_B target.

## Conviction
High.
